Round 433 — world-line branch axis: (fra.decision_summary: AI-authoring greenlit as the live consumer; the sec 7.10 build order executes, pull 1 of 3: the world-line branch axis (sec 7.9 axis 2) lands. A branch is which-world, a frame is who-believes - the four-axis decomposition holds and the two never conflate; per-branch ground truth is the (branch, frame) composite. NarrativeFact gains an additive branch field (serde default main, the default never serializes, so every pre-branch store round-trips byte-identical; schema v12->v13, declarative default, no migration arm). Guardrail B-2 executes exactly as written at R431: same_scope widens frame -> (frame, branch) as a key-widening, not a rewrite - same-frame facts on different world-lines never conflict (cross-branch pairs are data, like cross-frame pairs), and in-frame succession never crosses a branch (world-line divergence is data, not succession; enforced at the write path and re-checked by the scan). Canon order goes branch-relative (B-1 carried): canon-order/v1 gains an optional branches map of per-world-line edge sets, each composed with the shared base - the same quest node can legitimately order differently on two branches, which a single global DAG cannot express (it would be a cycle). frame_view scopes to one world-line (--branch, default main) behind a derived fail-loud unknown-branch boundary (no registry: known = main, fact-carried, or order-declared; a typo'd branch must not read as an empty world).
changes_bullets: NarrativeFact.branch in mnemosyne-core narrative.rs (+ MAIN_BRANCH const, re-exported): serde default "main", default never serializes - single-world stores round-trip byte-identical; schema v12->v13 (R413 declarative-default pattern, no migration arm)

FactImport.branch (Option; omitted = main, blank-after-trim rejected) routed through the ONE shared build_candidate_fact (R305 parity); validate_fact_refs widens succession to same (frame, branch) - cross-branch supersedes rejects on both write paths (divergence is data, not succession)

continuity gate B-2 key-widening executed as designed: same_scope = (frame, branch); ContinuityReport.cross_frame_pairs renamed cross_scope_pairs (cross-frame OR cross-branch = data, never gated); FrameConflictOverlap carries branch; new SuccessionCrossBranch violation (out-of-band-edit re-check, fail-loud)

canon-order/v1 gains optional branches{branch_id: edges[]} (lenient-additive): per-branch transitive closure of (shared base UNION branch edges), cycle-checked per composition; CanonOrder.le/comparable take the governing branch; a branch absent from the declaration orders by the base alone

frame_view(store, order, frame, branch, at) + FrameView.branch: a view never mixes world-lines; unknown branch fails loud via the derived known-set (main, fact-carried, order-declared) - no registry, but a typo'd branch must not read as an empty world
CLI: add-fact --branch, report-frame-view --branch (default main; json gains branch), validate-continuity json emits cross_scope_pairs + succession_cross_branch
verification_bullets: new unit tests: branch defaults to main + default-skip serialization (byte-stability), cross-branch succession rejected on BOTH write paths, blank-branch added to the R305 parity matrix, same-frame cross-branch conflict = data (zero violations), per-branch order gates each world-line under its own reversed order (ch-2/ch-3 ordered oppositely on two branches - inexpressible as one global DAG), frame_view world-line scoping + typo'd-branch fail-loud
CLI smoke end-to-end: add-fact --branch then report-frame-view --branch sees only that world-line, the default view never mixes it in, typo'd branch errors; import-facts smoke updated to v13
full workspace suite green (757 tests, 0 failed), clippy --all-targets clean
carry_forward_bullets: sec 7.10 build order continues: pull 2 = authorial retract/amend (+ fix the divergent-reject advice that wrongly recommends in-frame supersession for an authorial typo), pull 3 = MCP narrative verb mirror
no branch registry by design (derived fail-loud known-set instead); a registry entry with description becomes consumer-pull if branch-level metadata materializes
convergence-B index key (branch_id, entity_id, valid_from) already matches FactKey; the log schema now carries every field that projection will need

Round 434 — authorial retract/amend: axi.decision_summary: Pull 2 of the sec 7.10 build order: the authorial correction path lands. The four-axis decomposition (sec 7.9) separates in-world belief change (axis 1/3: in-frame supersession, predecessor stays in the log) from author-time correction (axis 4: the claim itself was mis-written and must stop existing or change in place). Before this round a typo was permanent - any revision of a written fact divergent-rejected with NO alternative path, and the reject message actively gave WRONG advice (recommending in-frame supersession, which fabricates an in-world belief change out of an authorial slip). Two primitives close the gap: retract_fact (remove an unreferenced fact; inbound conflict edges / succession pointers block it fail-loud with the referrer list, so scan invariants never see a dangling target; mandatory reason, the remove_section audit-safeguard precedent; no tombstone - transaction time is the git history of the log per R330) and amend_fact (in-place revision keeping the id, routed through the SAME shared builder as add_fact/import_facts per the R305 parity rule, with inbound successors required to stay same-(frame, branch) so succession scope cannot be corrupted). The divergent-reject messages now advise both paths by name.
changes_bullets: retract_fact(store, sidecar, fact_id, reason) in mnemosyne-atomic: removes a fact the AUTHOR no longer asserts; reason mandatory (remove_section precedent); fail-loud referential integrity - any inbound conflict edge / succession pointer blocks the retract with the referrer list (a recorded assertion never dangles); no tombstone in the store - the transaction-time audit is the git history of the log (R330)

amend_fact(store, sidecar, entry, reason) in mnemosyne-atomic: in-place authorial revision keeping the id; routes the SAME build_candidate_fact as add_fact/import_facts (R305 parity - one closed invariant set, quote_sha256 restamped, never caller-supplied); NotFound when absent (creation stays add_fact); byte-identical = no-op; inbound successors must stay same-(frame, branch) - an amend cannot move a superseded fact out of its scope

divergent-reject advice fixed (the sec 7.10 probe FINDING): add_fact and import_facts rejects now name BOTH paths - in-world belief change = supersede in-frame, authorial correction = amend_fact / retract_fact (the old message wrongly recommended supersession for a typo)
CLI: amend-fact (shares the add-fact flag set via one extracted parser + --reason) and retract-fact (--fact --reason) verbs; usage roster + per-verb help updated
verification_bullets: atomic unit tests: retract removes unreferenced + blocks referenced (with referrer named) + reason mandatory + NotFound; amend revises in place with quote hash restamped + no-op on identical + shared-builder invariant (empty evidence rejected on the amend path too) + scope-move blocked while superseded + divergent add advises both paths

CLI smoke (authorial_revision_smoke.rs): the typo-fix loop end-to-end - amend without --reason rejected, with --reason revises in place; divergent re-add stderr names both paths; retract demands reason then empties the store
full workspace suite green (761 tests, 0 failed), clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean

Round 435 — MCP narrative verb mirror: t.decision_summary: Pull 3 of the sec 7.10 build order, completing the gate set that round's verdict named: the narrative verbs reach MCP, the interface an authoring AI actually holds (R127 mutate-gate; the R430-R434 primitives were CLI-only, which the 7.10 probe flagged as a hard gap). Five write tools (add_frame, add_fact, add_fact_conflict, amend_fact, retract_fact) are mechanical mirrors over the SAME in-process atomic primitives the CLI calls - no new invariants, no second write path (R305 discipline holds: MCP args translate shape-only into FactImport and every rule stays in build_candidate_fact). Two read tools (validate_continuity, report_frame_view) close the authoring loop: write the scene, declare the facts, query the frame's in-effect beliefs for the next scene, gate continuity. Their order/severity resolution was extracted into mnemosyne-ops as the single shared chain (the R319 charter and the workspace_entry_id_prefix precedent) - the CLI's two duplicated resolution blocks collapse onto it, and the report types gain Serialize so both wires emit one byte-compatible JSON contract instead of two hand-built ones.
changes_bullets: 7 MCP tools in mnemosyne-mcp (roster 38 -> 45): add_frame / add_fact / add_fact_conflict / amend_fact / retract_fact (writes via run_atomic_mutate, the same in-process primitives as the CLI - zero new invariants) + validate_continuity / report_frame_view (reads)

shared read orchestration extracted to mnemosyne-ops (R319 charter): continuity_scan + continuity_frame_view + private resolve_canon_order (override > [continuity].canon_order_path + sha256 pin > equality-only; override bypasses the pin per the R428 rule) - ONE resolution chain for CLI and MCP, replacing the two duplicated match blocks in main.rs

ContinuityViolation/ContinuityReport/FrameView/FrameViewEntry gain Serialize (violation enum serde-tagged kind/snake_case, byte-compatible with the hand-built CLI json); CLI validate-continuity / report-frame-view now serialize the shared ops envelopes (ContinuityScanReport / FrameViewReport) instead of hand-assembling json
amend_fact MCP args flatten the add_fact shape + reason (one fact shape, two verbs); fact_import_from = pure shape translation, all invariants stay in build_candidate_fact
verification_bullets: live stdio handshake: tools/list returns 45 tools including all 7 narrative verbs; tools/call validate_continuity returns the JSON envelope (severity null / facts 0 on the dogfood store - gate opt-in, scan reported)

existing validate_continuity + report_frame_view CLI smoke suites pass unchanged against the serde-serialized envelopes - the json contract survived the refactor byte-compatibly (kind tags, field names, holding_count)
full workspace suite green (761 tests, 0 failed), clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean

Round 436 — branch registry: frames-regi.decision_summary: Session-review finding executed: the R433 minimal pin left branches as free-form strings - frame refs failed loud against a registry while a typo'd branch silently created a world, and the read-side derived known-set (main / fact-carried / order-declared) could not catch write-side typos because the typo'd fact itself made the branch look known. The asymmetry had no principled justification; under lifetime-project standards the registry is the textbook shape (the same R-1 / R416 fail-loud-refs reasoning that gave frames their registry applies verbatim to world-lines). This round lands the Branch entity + AtomicStore.branches (v13->v14), write-path enforcement in the ONE shared builder, an add_branch primitive mirrored through CLI and MCP, and a single shared declaration<->store boundary for the gate and the view (order nodes must be sections; declared branch edge sets must be registered; declaring the default branch rejects - the base edges ARE its order). MAIN_BRANCH stays known by construction, never registered: the default axis value exists structurally, and requiring its registration would invalidate every existing single-world store. The R433 derived known-set heuristic is deleted - this round also pays two doc drifts the review surfaced (FactKey.branch_id now correctly documented as hosting the world-line branch, not the frame; the append-only genre doc names its axis-4 exception) and folds the duplicate config discovery in ops.
changes_bullets: Branch entity (mnemosyne-core) + AtomicStore.branches registry, schema v13->v14 (declarative serde default, no migration arm; a single-world store loads and gates exactly as before); MAIN_BRANCH is known by construction and REJECTS registration (one way to say the default)

build_candidate_fact: a non-default fact branch must be registered (fail-loud on both write paths via the shared builder - R305 parity holds with zero extra work); FactsManifest gains branches[] (registries land before facts, one atomic transaction); receipt summary now counts branches

frame_view: the R433 derived known-set heuristic (main / fact-carried / order-declared) is REMOVED, replaced by the registry lookup; check_order_against_store = ONE shared boundary for gate and view (order nodes must be sections AND declared branch edge sets must name registered world-lines); canon-order branches{} declaring the default branch rejects (base edges ARE its order)
add_branch primitive + add-branch CLI verb + add_branch MCP tool (roster 45->46), all A2-verdict consistent (absent create / identical no-op / divergent reject)

doc drift fixed: narrative.rs module doc now maps the WORLD-LINE branch (not the frame) onto FactKey.branch_id per sec 7.9 axis 2; NarrativeFact struct doc names the axis-4 amend/retract exception to the append-only genre
ops: continuity_scan/continuity_frame_view fold to ONE discover_config via the ContinuityPolicy view (was two discoveries per scan)
verification_bullets: atomic unit tests: add_branch full verdict matrix (main rejects registration, divergent rejects, no-op idempotent, registration unlocks the fact write), unregistered branch added to the R305 parity matrix (both write paths reject identically)

continuity unit tests: order declaring an unregistered branch fails loud through BOTH the gate and the view (shared boundary), branches{main} declaration rejects, typo'd view branch errors with the registry message

branch lifecycle beyond registration (description setter, remove/merge) = consumer-pull, symmetric with frames

Round 437 — entity axis: the third regis.decision_summary: Design sec 7.10 gap 4 pulled live by a named consumer: pinion (AI-first game engine) will read narrative state over RPC, and entity-scoped verification - does this scene contradict X's background - needs a retrieval key that flat claims lack. The Entity entity lands as the third registry (frames R430, branches R436, entities now), with the same fail-loud contract: every NarrativeFact.entities ref must be registered, so a typo'd entity cannot silently split a dossier. Multi-ref by design (a relation involves two or more entities; world-level facts carry none), validated in the one shared builder so both write paths and amend inherit the invariant unchanged (R305). Two reads land with it: the entity dossier (all facts about X across frames and branches - raw authoring-time view) and the entity filter on the frame-at-T projection (frame x branch x entity at T - exactly the NPC-context query an AI-first runtime issues: what does this character know about X right now). kind stays a free-form consumer-defined tag - character/location/faction are game vocabulary, and nothing medium-shaped enters L0 (sec 6.4). This is also typed-claim step 1: the subject leg of the future rule-gate (predicate facts) now exists; the predicate leg remains the recorded design round.
changes_bullets: Entity entity (mnemosyne-core: kind + description, both free-form consumer-defined - nothing medium-shaped, sec 6.4) + AtomicStore.entities registry, schema v14->v15 (declarative default; entity-less facts serialize no key - byte-stable round-trip)

NarrativeFact.entities: Vec<String> (multi-ref by design - a relation involves >= 2 entities; optional - world-level facts carry none); every ref validated in the ONE shared builder: registered, non-blank, no duplicates (R305 parity free; "unregistered entity" added to the parity matrix)
add_entity primitive + manifest entities[] (registries-before-facts atomic transaction) + add-entity CLI verb + add_entity MCP tool, all A2-verdict consistent
report-entity / entity_dossier (ops shared): every fact referencing X across all frames and branches - the raw authoring-time "all facts about X" view, fail-loud on a typo'd entity

frame_view gains the entity filter (frame x branch x entity at T = the NPC-context query for the pinion AI-first runtime): --entity on report-frame-view CLI, entity arg on report_frame_view MCP; FrameViewEntry carries entities
verification_bullets: atomic unit tests: unregistered ref fails loud then registration unlocks the write, duplicate refs reject, entity-less fact serializes no entities key, registry round-trip + divergent/no-op verdicts; parity matrix extended
continuity unit test: entity filter scopes the view (2 holding -> 1 with filter), entity echoed on FrameView, typo'd filter fails loud
CLI smoke (entity_dossier_smoke.rs): register -> author -> dossier (kind + fact_count + rows) -> filtered frame-view (unknown surfaced under no order, B-1 honesty) -> typo'd dossier read fails loud
full workspace suite green (767 tests, 0 failed), clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean; MCP roster 46 -> 48 (add_entity, report_entity)
carry_forward_bullets: entity kind is a free-form tag by design (medium-neutral); a kind-scoped listing verb (all entities of kind K) is consumer-pull when the dossier workflow demonstrates it
entity lifecycle beyond registration (description/kind setter, remove with inbound-ref check) = consumer-pull, symmetric with frames/branches

entities are the typed-claim step 1 (subject typing); the rule-gate class (location exclusivity, alive/dead, conservation) still needs predicate-shaped claims - the recorded design round, do NOT improvise it ad hoc
next per the agreed order: branch fork-point (shared-history semantics - the session-review tension 1), then conflict-edge claim-hash drift (tension 2)

Round 438 — branch fork-point: shared-hi.decision_summary: The R433 branch axis shipped scoping without divergence semantics - the design's own justification for branch != frame was copy-on-write shared history, yet a branch view was as sparse as a frame: a story forking at chapter 30 lost chapters 1-29 on the branch view, the gate under-detected contradictions against inherited facts, and the module-doc claim that the log carries everything the index needs was false without a fork datum. This round closes that tension the way the four-axis decomposition wants it closed: Branch.forks_from records WHERE a world-line diverged (forest by construction - parents must pre-exist, forks are immutable), visibility becomes a three-state world-membership judgment (inherited-In / definitively-Out / order-cannot-decide-Unknown, surfaced never gated), holds_at evaluates in the QUERY world with successor visibility filtering (a fork's revision of an inherited belief is in-world change that never leaks back into the ancestor's view), conflicts evaluate in the pair's JOIN world (siblings share no world = data), succession may point along the lineage, and a branch's composed canon order inherits every ancestor's declared edges. forks_from = None preserves the standalone-world semantics exactly - every pre-fork store and test gates identically. For the pinion AI-first runtime this is the primitive that makes playthrough-as-branch and route-divergence authoring representable.
changes_bullets: Branch.forks_from{branch, at} (mnemosyne-core BranchFork), schema v15->v16; None = standalone world (R433 semantics preserved exactly); parent must pre-exist + fork immutable after registration = ancestry is a forest BY CONSTRUCTION, no cycle writable; build_branch_fork = the one fork-invariant place shared by add_branch and the manifest path

world visibility (3-state, B-1 honest): a fact on an ancestor branch is In iff canon_from <= that lineage's departure point, Out when comparable-and-after, Unknown when the declared order cannot decide (surfaced, never gated); holds_at is now world-relative and a successor ends a predecessor ONLY where the successor itself is visible - a fork's revision never leaks back into the ancestor's view

conflict scoping generalizes to the JOIN world: same frame + one branch on the other's lineage = evaluated in the deeper world (violation names it); siblings/unrelated = cross-scope data; succession may point along the lineage (write path + scan agree) - revising an inherited belief is in-world change

per-branch order composes the full ancestry: branch reach = closure(base + every ancestor's declared edges + own), cycle-checked per composition; load_canon_order now returns the FILE and construction happens post-store-load (ops compose_canon_order, single path for CLI/MCP)
CLI add-branch --forks-from/--forks-at + MCP add_branch fork args; lineage_of/fork_ancestry hop-capped fail-loud against out-of-band cyclic edits
verification_bullets: new semantics tests: fork inherits pre-fork facts but not later main facts (and main view unaffected), conflict with an inherited fact gates in the join world while sibling forks stay data, lineage succession revises without leak-back (route sees f-new, main still sees f-old), undecidable fork comparison surfaces as unknown, grandchild inherits ancestor branch order (deep gets route's edges, main does not)

atomic write-path tests: fork parent must pre-exist / no self-fork / fork point must be a section / immutable after registration (divergent reject) / lineage succession accepted while standalone-branch succession still rejects
ALL pre-fork tests pass unchanged - standalone-world semantics byte-preserved; full workspace suite green (773 tests, 0 failed), clippy clean, fmt clean
carry_forward_bullets: session-review tension 1 CLOSED: the log now carries the fork datum the CoW projection needs - the "log carries every field the index will ever need" claim holds again; convergence-B branch dimension maps onto this ancestry
playthrough-as-branch (pinion save slots) is now representable; runtime read scale stays flat-scan until convergence-B pulls

gate conservatism note: an Unknown-visibility successor does not cut its predecessor (false-negative direction, consistent with B-1 unordered-never-gated); revisit only if a consumer shows a real miss
next: conflict-edge claim-hash drift (tension 2), then the typed-claim/rule-gate design round

Round 439 — conflict-edge claim pins: st.decision_summary: A conflict edge is a recorded SEMANTIC judgment about claim content (sec 7.4) - but R434's amend could change the judged text out from under the judgment, leaving the gate evaluating an assertion about words that no longer exist. This round applies the codebase's own drift discipline (R404 content pins, R420 drift->Stale) to the judgment layer: every ConflictAssertion pins the target's claim sha256 at judgment time, computed by the primitives and never caller-supplied; the scan compares pins against current claims and surfaces ConflictEdgeStale demanding re-affirmation. The re-affirmation path is the amend itself - amending a fact restamps its OUTBOUND judgments (the amender freshly asserts its own edges) while INBOUND judgments go stale, which is exactly the asymmetry the axis-4 model wants: you can re-state what you believe, you cannot silently keep others' statements about your old text. Clean break v16->v17 with no compat shim (pre-release; no committed store carries an edge; the old shape fails loud). Succession deliberately stays unpinned: it relates fact identities, not wordings.
changes_bullets: NarrativeFact.conflicts_with: bare target ids -> ConflictAssertion{target, target_claim_sha256} (mnemosyne-core), schema v16->v17 - CLEAN BREAK, no compat shim (pre-release rule; no committed consumer store carries an edge; an old-shape store fails to load LOUDLY rather than silently dropping the pin)

the pin is computed by the primitives, never caller-supplied (R404): validate_fact_refs becomes validate_and_stamp_fact_refs (the one shared pass - add_fact / import_facts post-staging snapshot / amend_fact all route it); add_fact_conflict stamps from the target's current claim; FactImport keeps bare ids (the authoring face is unchanged)
amend semantics: amending a fact RESTAMPS its outbound judgments (the amender freshly asserts its own edges); inbound judgments pointing AT the amended fact go stale instead - never auto-refreshed

scan_continuity surfaces ConflictEdgeStale{fact_id, target, stamped_sha256, current_sha256}; the pair is still evaluated (explicit conservatism both ways: staleness gates under reject, the overlap check is not suppressed)
validate_continuity smoke fixture now records its edge through the real primitive instead of hand-written JSON (the pin cannot be hand-authored)
verification_bullets: drift round-trip unit test: fresh stamp = 0 stale; amend the TARGET -> exactly 1 ConflictEdgeStale; amend the edge-owning fact (same content) -> outbound restamp -> 0 stale (the re-affirmation path works end-to-end)
add_fact_conflict test extended: stored assertion carries target + 64-hex pin equal to sha256(target claim)
import smoke asserts the staged-manifest edge is stamped through the post-staging snapshot pass; full workspace suite green (774 tests, 0 failed), clippy clean, fmt clean
carry_forward_bullets: session-review tension 2 CLOSED: recorded semantic judgments can no longer be silently invalidated by an amend - the drift->Stale shape (R404/R420) now covers conflict edges
a dedicated re-affirm primitive (re-stamp one edge without a full amend) = consumer-pull if the amend-same-content idiom shows friction

supersedes_in_frame deliberately NOT content-pinned: succession is an in-world relation between fact IDENTITIES (the successor replaces the belief whatever its exact wording), not a judgment about specific text - pin only where the judgment is about content

remaining from the AAA map: typed-claim/rule-gate design round (class C), setup/payoff coverage, story-time axis, LLM discovery adapter, pinion adapter (statechart->canon-order, scene-script bindings)

Round 440 — hardening: one fork walk, to.decision_summary: The second session review held R433-R439 to the lifetime-project standard and found five defects; this round pays all of them plus one live bug the fix surfaced. The deep one was a fail-loud asymmetry: the scan re-checked conflict/succession targets against out-of-band edits while trusting frame/branch/entity registry membership and section refs entirely to the write path - and the lineage lookup PANICKED on an unregistered fact branch instead of erroring. The shared gate/view boundary now re-checks the store's full referential closure (an Err, because a store that fails it is corrupt and the semantics are not evaluable - distinct from missing conflict targets, which remain evaluable data findings), making every downstream lineage lookup total by invariant. The fork-chain traversal existed in three subtly-different copies across two crates - exactly where drift bugs breed - and is now ONE core function all paths route. Fixing the review findings surfaced a real R439 bug: import_facts verdicted before stamping, so an idempotent re-import of a manifest with conflict edges false-diverged on empty pins - stamping now precedes verdicts on both write paths (R305 parity restored, regression-tested). Round 440 — hardening: one fork walk, to.publishable_decision_summary: The second session review held R433-R439 to the lifetime-project standard and found five defects; this round pays all of them plus one live bug the fix surfaced. Round 441 — typed-claim/rule-gate design.decision_summary: The recorded design round R437 deferred (do-not-improvise carry honored). The typed leg: NarrativeFact gains an OPTIONAL authored TypedClaim{subject: entity-id, predicate: registered-id, object: Entity(entity-id)|Value(scalar)} beside the required prose claim - B-1 applied to typing (declared at write time in the same act as the prose, never NLP-inferred), binary predicates only, subject and entity-object must be registered AND members of the fact's entities list (the retrieval key is never silently widened). Partial coverage IS the design: probed on the 10 real Dracula claims, 3 type cleanly, 5 are compound prose where typing forces the per-claim split the prose tolerated, 2 are negative-epistemic and stay prose-only (open-world: absence = unrecorded, never false) - the deterministic gate covers the typed subset while recorded edges and the future LLM-discovery adapter cover the rest, three conflict sources one gate. Predicates are load-bearing refs (rules key off them; a typo'd predicate silently escapes its rule - the R436 write-side-typo lesson), so they become the 4th fail-loud registry carrying object_kind entity|scalar for builder shape-checking; per the R440 carry, build Round A performs the registry dedup in the same change that adds the 4th. Rules are consumer vocabulary and never enter L0: a narrative-rules/v1 declared artifact (the canon-order/R428 authority-input pattern, optional sha256 pin) with exactly TWO classes covering the named trio - exclusive{predicate, per: subject|object} (location exclusivity per subject, conservation/custody per object; same-value co-holding duplicates are consistent, not gated) and transition{predicate, allowed} riding the in-frame succession edge (succession IS the declared adjacency - adjacency over a partial canon order is ill-defined; unchained same-subject/predicate visible pairs are surfaced as a WORLD-scoped count, never gated). Evaluation lands inside scan_continuity as new violation variants and MUST reuse WorldCtx::holds_at verbatim as its third reader - the probe's binding finding: a paper interval model with an inclusive successor boundary produced 4 false positives on correctly chained real arcs; the half-open successor cut is load-bearing. Frame x branch scoping carries unchanged (cross-frame and fork-world pairs proved inert on real data), succession chains become structurally rewarded (a forgotten chain is now a caught overlap), and rule findings are derivations re-evaluated each scan - no staleness, no pins (judgments keep their R439 pins). Opt-in = authoring the rules file; violations ride the existing continuity severity knob. Design only: no schema or production code this round; build = Round A then Round B, consumer-pulled.
changes_bullets: design doc sec 7.12 (claudedocs/fictional-adapter-design.md): TypedClaim{subject: entity-id, predicate: registered-id, object: Entity|Value} as an optional authored leg beside the prose claim; predicate registry as the 4th fail-loud registry with object_kind shape check; narrative-rules/v1 consumer-declared artifact with TWO rule classes (exclusive{predicate, per: subject|object} + transition{predicate, allowed} riding succession edges); scan_continuity integration via new violation variants; non-goal revisit triggers; build decomposition Round A/B

executable PoC probe (claudedocs/phase1-novel-poc/rule-gate-probe.py): mirrors holds_at/visibility/fork semantics over real Dracula arcs (Dracula location chain castle-ship-england-castle, Lucy life-status alive-dead-undead-destroyed, journal custody jonathan-to-mina) plus fabricated violations and cross-frame/fork counterchecks
no store schema change and no production code this round (design only; the store mutation is this entry)
verification_bullets: probe 1: correctly chained real arcs green including the A-B-A location revisit; probes 2-4: forgotten location chain, destroyed-to-alive transition, double custody each caught at the exact offending pair

probes 5-6: cross-frame (jonathan's castle belief vs ground-truth england) and fork-world (alive-lucy on a lucy-lives fork vs dead on main) both inert - frames/worlds never cross-validated carries into the rule class unchanged

probe 7 typability survey over the 10 real facts.json claims: 3 cleanly typeable / 5 partial (compound prose - typing forces the per-claim split, F08's three state changes become the S01..S04 chain) / 2 untypeable (negative-epistemic, open-world out of scope) - partial coverage confirmed as the design, not a gap
the first paper model was falsified by the probe itself: an inclusive successor boundary produced 4 false positives on correct data, folded back as the binding reuse-holds_at finding
carry_forward_bullets: build rounds land consumer-pulled (pinion): Round A = TypedClaim + predicate registry + the 4-registry dedup (R440 carry trigger fires) + schema v17-to-v18 + parity + CLI/MCP mirror; Round B = narrative-rules/v1 loader (sha256 pin) + gate evaluation + unchained_state_pairs surfacing + [continuity] rules_path; design frozen here - do not improvise drift at build time

Round 442 — setup/payoff coverage: Chekh.decision_summary: The AAA-map item carried a name and no definition; this round derived one from two existing patterns and pinned it: narrative craft's setup/payoff discipline (an introduced device must be used) expressed in this codebase's coverage vocabulary (R389/R390 - a declared expectation plus a read-only 3-way classification), probed on real Dracula arcs BEFORE building. A fact opts in as a setup via payoff_expectation=expected (default unmarked - absence is unrecorded, never an assertion, the sparse-frame ethos on the discourse axis; the default never serializes so pre-payoff stores stay byte-stable); a later fact resolves it via pays_off backward identity edges, the supersedes_in_frame pointer shape - the setup is written first and never touched when paid, append-only by genre. Edges are deliberately UNPINNED (the R439 content pin covers judgments about claim text; succession and payoff relate fact identities, not wordings) and cross FRAMES freely (a discourse-structure relation, not an epistemic judgment - Renfield's zoophagy is set up in seward's frame and paid off in ground truth) but never credit across worlds. The report classifies per query world over the shared visibility semantics: paid / dangling / exempt plus three honesty surfaces (payoffs_to_unmarked = forgotten marking, payoff_before_setup = mystery structure surfaced never gated, unknown = B-1 undecidable). The probe's central finding drove the scoping: an inherited setup DANGLES on a fork until that world-line itself pays it - main's post-fork payoff is invisible there, so forking early surfaces all the narrative debt the new world inherits; each playthrough resolves its own guns. Dangling is deliberately never gated - a WIP story has dangling setups by definition; the dangling list IS the author's todo list, and a release-time no-dangling gate stays consumer-pull. Write-side invariants ride the one shared builder path (R305): tag parse, no self/blank/duplicate refs, fail-loud targets, inbound pays_off blocks retraction; the scan re-checks out-of-band edits as PayoffTargetMissing, an evaluable finding rather than a corruption Err per the R440 boundary doctrine.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-core: PayoffExpectation{unmarked default, expected} + NarrativeFact.payoff_expectation (never serialized when default - pre-payoff stores byte-stable) + pays_off backward edge on the payoff fact (the supersedes_in_frame pointer shape: the setup is never touched when paid); schema v17-to-v18

shared builder + validate_and_stamp_fact_refs (R305 one path): tag must parse, no self/blank/duplicate pays_off, targets must exist - UNPINNED identity refs (the R439 pin covers claim-text judgments; succession and payoff relate identities); inbound pays_off blocks retract_fact like conflict/succession refs; scan_continuity re-checks out-of-band edits as PayoffTargetMissing (evaluable finding, not corruption Err - R440 boundary doctrine)

payoff_coverage read projection in mnemosyne-validate + ops::payoff_coverage_report (shared order resolution) + report-payoff-coverage CLI verb (TTY + --json) + report_payoff_coverage MCP mirror (49 tools) + add-fact/amend-fact --payoff-expectation/--pays-off flags (CLI + MCP arg parity)
verification_bullets: executable probe (claudedocs/phase1-novel-poc/payoff-coverage-probe.py) on real Dracula arcs BEFORE building: Renfield zoophagy (cross-frame payoff), Jonathan's journal (multi-payoff), bowie knife at a WIP cut (dangling), garlic guard across a lucy-lives fork - all four shapes confirmed the schema

Round 443 — session review of R441-R442:.decision_summary: The R436/R440 cadence applied to the two newest rounds: hold R441 (typed-claim/rule-gate design) and R442 (setup/payoff coverage) to the lifetime-project standard before anything builds on them. Three findings, all repaid in-session. The real one is in the R441 probe: the exclusive rule's consistency skip ('a restated fact is not a violation') was implemented one-sided - it skipped equal OBJECTS under per:subject but never skipped equal SUBJECTS under per:object, so a restated custody fact (the same holder stated twice) would false-positive as a two-holder conservation violation. The principle is now pinned symmetric - the skip applies to the NON-KEYED leg, both directions - in the probe and in design sec 7.12, so build round B inherits the rule, not the bug. The review also caught the reviewer: the first regression fixture left the restated fact's extent open, which IS a genuine two-holder conflict with the later custody transfer, and the gate correctly flagged it - the fixture was wrong, not the gate; extent closed. Finding 2 is an R442 omission: a payoff edge whose endpoints no world ever sees together (sibling-branch endpoints, both existing) was silent dead data - the setup's world shows dangling (the symptom) but nothing surfaced the edge itself, so an author who paid a gun on the wrong world-line got no signal that the edge credits nowhere. PayoffCoverageReport.uncredited_edges now lists edges that credited in no world, with B-1 honesty (an edge touching an undecidable-visibility endpoint is withdrawn, not declared dead). A write-side reject for this is impossible by design: world-coherence depends on the declared canon order, a validate-time artifact the mutate primitive never sees. Finding 3: the pays_off forward-ref-within-manifest path (the succession symmetry) was untested; covered. DEBT-1 (ARCHITECTURE doc drift) was checked on the way in and verified already paid.

A write-side reject for this is impossible by design: world-coherence depends on the declared canon order, a validate-time artifact the mutate primitive never sees. Round 444 — second corpus ratified: Detr.decision_summary: The owner challenged the consumer-gate framing - the engine must be a complete narrative engine without pinion - and the challenge surfaced a real R441 framing error: the typed-claim build gate was recorded as 'pinion pull' when the correct gate is 'a corpus at the scale where recorded conflict edges stop scaling'; pinion was only the likely first such consumer. The resolution is the owner's gate-opening act (the R429 ratification precedent): declare the second corpus that supplies the data the gated work was waiting for. Honest constraint first: no public-domain branching novel exists - the gamebook/CYOA genre postdates the public-domain horizon - so the corpus is a documented game branch structure, hand-extracted as freshly-authored facts (plot facts are not copyrightable; the Dracula hand-extraction precedent carries). PRIMARY: Detroit: Become Human's branch structure, chosen because it stresses every built axis with real data at once - three protagonist frames with genuine knowledge asymmetry (the first corpus where frames are concurrent protagonists, not observers of one plot), a genuine DAG canon order (B-1's partial-order machinery has never met real non-linear data; Dracula was a chain), named world-line fork points with shared history (R438's first external stress), mid-story character deaths with the narrative continuing (live alive/dead state chains = the R441 rule-gate's scale pull arriving as data), and cross-chapter consequence locks (R442 setup/payoff and uncredited-edge surfaces exercised across worlds). SECONDARY: inkle's The Intercept (MIT verified, machine-readable .ink branch source) as the ink-to-canon-order converter spike - a dry run of the pinion adapter contract shape against an actual open branch graph; it validates the contract, not scale. Gate disposition: typed-claim Rounds A/B proceed PoC-first on the Detroit slice; story-time stays gated (no flashback pull in Detroit); LLM discovery stays scale-gated until extracted fact count makes recorded edges fail in practice. Ratification only - no code this round.

Round 445 — Detroit PoC slice: real bran.decision_summary: Build order step 1 of R444 executed in a fresh session: The Hostage + Stormy Night flowcharts (plus a thin A New Home spine for the cross-chapter gun/window setups) hand-extracted from public flowchart documentation into a scratch store under claudedocs/phase1-detroit-poc/, existing primitives only, code 0 lines. Every ratified stress axis held semantically live: concurrent-protagonist frames with real knowledge asymmetry, a genuine DAG canon order under per-branch composed edge sets, fork-of-fork shared-history inheritance with world-scoped succession closure, cross-branch supersession of one main fact by two sibling worlds, per-world setup/payoff with the R442 inherited-setup-dangles finding reproduced on external data, and the continuity gate firing on a deliberately injected same-(frame x world) overlap then returning green after retraction. Five findings recorded verbatim in poc-report.md: the inclusive fork boundary yields a binding authoring convention (forks_at = last shared beat - a deviancy fact leaked into the stay-machine world until the fork moved to the dinner scene); the branch registry has no amend/retract so a mis-declared fork point forces a store rebuild; the R443 uncredited_edges surface withdraws an edge globally when ANY world sees an endpoint as Unknown, which silently masks genuinely dead cross-chain edges under parallel protagonist chains (falsified live: the same store under a discourse-interleave order surfaces the dead edge exactly as designed); reconvergent flag-carrying branch graphs are not tree world-lines - the gun flag forced either an early fork that story-falsely loses the later deviancy beat, per-world fact cloning, or world-per-flag-combo explosion, which is R444's scale-gate condition arriving as data and sharpens the typed-claim rationale (carried flags are typed state, not narrative divergence); and the canon-order declaration is an adapter decision with material honesty-surface effects. Verdict: typed-claim pull CONFIRMED - Round A/B proceed PoC-first on the Detroit store, the Finding-3 fix lands in the same window, branch amend/retract queues on the AI-authoring pull list.

Round 446 — typed-claim Round A: TypedCl.decision_summary: The R441-frozen typed-claim design built exactly as designed, against the R444/R445-opened data pull: the optional TypedClaim leg (subject-predicate-object, binary only, authored in the same act as the prose claim - never NLP-derived, B-1 applied to typing) lands on NarrativeFact with the Predicate registry as the FOURTH fail-loud registry (predicates are load-bearing refs - rules key off them, the R436 write-side-typo lesson - in contrast with the deliberately free-form Entity.kind), and per the R440 carry trigger the same change performs the registry-code dedup: one shared staging path (A2 3-way verdict: absent create / byte-identical no-op / divergent reject) replaces six hand-rolled copies across the standalone primitives and the import_facts loops. All typed invariants live in one builder site shared by both fact write paths (R305 parity, paired with a 10-case parity test): subject and entity-shaped objects must be registered entities AND members of the fact's entities list (the entities list stays THE retrieval key), the predicate must be registered, and the object leg's shape must match the predicate's declared object_kind (entity | scalar - scalar values are opaque consumer vocabulary, never enumerated by the substrate). Schema v18 to v19 with declarative serde defaults: pre-v19 stores load unchanged, prose-only facts serialize no typed key. Surface: add-predicate CLI verb + all-or-nothing typed flags on add-fact/amend-fact, MCP add_predicate + typed arg object (50 tools), typed legs surfaced verbatim in report-frame-view/report-entity. Dogfooded live on the Detroit slice store: 4 predicates + 14 typed legs in one import transaction covering the slice's five state chains (operational/alive/deviancy/custody), plus the amend-typing pass on an existing prose fact. Partial coverage is the design: compound prose types one leg, untypeable claims stay prose-only; the deterministic gate over the typed subset arrives in Round B (rules artifact + scan evaluation, design frozen).
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-core narrative.rs: TypedClaim{subject, predicate, object: Entity{id} | Value{value}} (serde-tagged, no stringly union), Predicate{object_kind: entity|scalar, description}, PredicateObjectKind with as_str/from_tag (CoverageExpectation symmetry); NarrativeFact.typed Option<TypedClaim> skip-serialized - prose-only facts stay byte-stable

mnemosyne-atomic: AtomicStore.predicates BTreeMap (4th registry), schema v18 -> v19 declarative serde default (no migration arm); build_typed_claim = the ONE typed-leg invariant site shared by both fact write paths (subject + entity-object must be registered AND members of the fact entities list, predicate registered, object shape matches the declared object_kind); add_predicate primitive + manifest predicates[] via shared build_predicate tag parse

4-registry dedup (the R440 carry trigger fired): stage_registry_entry + registry_receipt collapse six hand-rolled A2 3-way verdict copies (add_frame/add_branch/add_entity + the three import_facts loops); registry-specific prechecks (MAIN_BRANCH reject, fork shaping, object_kind parse) stay with callers

Round 446 — typed-claim Round A: TypedCl.publishable_decision_summary: The R441-frozen typed-claim design built exactly as designed, against the R444/R445-opened data pull: the optional TypedClaim leg (subject-predicate-object, binary only, authored in the same act as the prose claim - never NLP-derived, B-1 applied to typing) lands on NarrativeFact with the Predicate registry as the FOURTH fail-loud registry (predicates are load-bearing refs - rules key off them, the R436 write-side-typo lesson - in contrast with the deliberately free-form Entity.kind), and per the R440 carry trigger the same change performs the registry-code dedup: one shared staging path (A2 3-way verdict: absent create / byte-identical no-op / divergent reject) replaces six hand-rolled copies across the standalone primitives and the import_facts loops. All typed invariants live in one builder site shared by both fact write paths (R305 parity, paired with a 10-case parity test): subject and entity-shaped objects must be registered entities AND members of the fact's entities list (the entities list stays THE retrieval key), the predicate must be registered, and the object leg's shape must match the predicate's declared object_kind (entity | scalar - scalar values are opaque consumer vocabulary, never enumerated by the substrate). Schema v18 to v19 with declarative serde defaults: pre-v19 stores load unchanged, prose-only facts serialize no typed key. Surface: add-predicate CLI verb + all-or-nothing typed flags on add-fact/amend-fact, MCP add_predicate + typed arg object (50 tools), typed legs surfaced verbatim in report-frame-view/report-entity. Dogfooded live on the Detroit slice store: 4 predicates + 14 typed legs in one import transaction covering the slice's five state chains (operational/alive/deviancy/custody), plus the amend-typing pass on an existing prose fact. Partial coverage is the design: compound prose types one leg, untypeable claims stay prose-only; the deterministic gate over the typed subset arrives in Round B (rules artifact + scan evaluation, design frozen).

Round 447 — payoff dead-edge masking fix.decision_summary: The R445 Detroit slice's Finding 3 closed: the R443 uncredited_edges surface withdrew an edge from the global dead-edge pool whenever ANY world saw EITHER endpoint as Unknown - and since parallel protagonist chains make every cross-chain fact Unknown in the other protagonist's forks, the surface went structurally silent on exactly the corpus shape that ratified it (R445 falsified this live: the genuinely dead F-K13 -> F-K05 edge was masked under the parallel-chains order and surfaced only under a discourse interleave). The fix scopes the withdrawal to worlds where the edge COULD credit: both endpoints In or Unknown with at least one Unknown - a world where either endpoint is Out is decided (the edge cannot credit there) and exempts nothing. Edges that credit nowhere but met a genuine could-credit undecidable world now surface as a new undecidable_edges report field instead of silently draining (the no-silent-caps rule: silent withdrawal reads as 'no dead edges' when it means 'could not decide'), and they are deliberately never listed as definitively dead (B-1 honesty both directions). Verified on the live Detroit store: the parallel-chains order and the discourse-interleave order now agree on the dead edge - the order declaration choice (R445 Finding 5) no longer changes whether a dead edge is visible, only how much becomes undecidable.
changes_bullets: continuity.rs payoff_coverage: the per-world withdrawal of never-credited edges now requires a COULD-CREDIT undecidable world - both endpoints In/Unknown with at least one Unknown; a world where either endpoint is Out is decided and exempts nothing (pre-fix: any world's Unknown on either endpoint withdrew the edge globally, so under parallel protagonist chains every dead cross-chain edge was silently masked by Unknowns in unrelated forks)

new PayoffCoverageReport.undecidable_edges surface: edges that credited nowhere AND met a could-credit undecidable world - surfaced instead of silently dropped (no-silent-caps), explicitly not listed as definitively dead (B-1); TTY rendering prints [UNDECIDABLE EDGE] beside [UNCREDITED EDGE]

two synthesized regression fixtures (R404, Detroit stays local): dead-edge-not-masked (payoff Out + setup Unknown in a parallel-chain fork = decided world, edge surfaces uncredited) and could-credit-unknown (payoff In + setup Unknown in one world = undecidable_edges, not uncredited); store_with_forks helper gains a parallel k-* section chain
verification_bullets: the R445 falsification pair now closes both ways on the live Detroit slice store: under the parallel-chains canon-order (where R445 demonstrated the silent mask) the dead F-K13 -> F-K05 edge NOW surfaces as uncredited_edges, identical to the discourse-interleave order's verdict; undecidable_edges stays empty there (every Unknown-touching world has the payoff endpoint Out = decided)

Round 448 — session review of R445-R447:.decision_summary: Adversarial session review of the Detroit slice + typed-claim Round A + dead-edge fix window, under the owner's standing standard (textbook structure over cost, lifetime-project bar). Verdict first: the architectural spine held - schema v19 is additive serde-default with no migration arm, the typed leg is authored-never-derived (B-1), all typed invariants live in one shared builder path (R305), the 4-registry dedup removed duplication rather than adding abstraction, reports stay derived-fresh projections over the log SSOT, and no finding touches the North-Star axes (frames never cross-validated, worlds never merged, canon order declared never inferred). Six findings, all repaid in-round: a three-state visibility re-encoded as Option<bool> beside the existing Vis enum (one-representation smell, fixed); the flattened typed-object arg resolution hand-rolled twice across CLI and MCP surfaces (collapsed into TypedObject::from_exclusive_args - the half-enforced-invariant lesson applied to arg surfaces); a precise-failure regression from the R446 dedup (blank branch id with a fork declaration errored with a blank-named fork message; fail-fast restored); a LATENT lost-update race in mnemosyne-mcp surfaced by this review's live concurrent-burst probe - parallel tools/call mutates ran unserialized load->save on one store file (real: MCP clients issue parallel calls); fixed with a server-level mutate lock behind a single run_mutate entry, burst-verified 0 errors with all writes present; design-doc sec 7.13 drift (still described the R443 blanket exclusion R447 superseded); and a test-count honesty correction (prior 795/797 figures were a summation artifact; canonical 792 -> 793). The review also closed R446's verification gap by running the live MCP stdio check (50 tools, typed leg persisted end-to-end) and re-ran the full Detroit battery green with both order declarations agreeing.
changes_bullets: finding 1 (code smell): the R447 withdrawal loop re-encoded three-state visibility as Option<bool> beside the existing Vis enum in the same file - replaced with a per-world vis_by_id map reusing Vis (the R440 one-representation discipline)

finding 2 (one-place rule): the flattened typed-object arg pair (entity vs value) was resolved by hand-rolled copies in BOTH the CLI flag parser and the MCP arg mapper - collapsed into TypedObject::from_exclusive_args in core (the one place the exactly-one rule lives) + unit test; both surfaces now emit the identical message

finding 3 (precise failure regression): the R446 registry dedup moved the blank-branch-id check after fork shaping, so a blank id WITH a fork declaration errored with a blank-named fork message - blank-id fail-fast restored in add_branch and the manifest loop with regression assertions

finding 4 (latent race, surfaced by this review's live burst probe): mnemosyne-mcp served concurrent tools/call mutates with unserialized load->save on one store file - lost-update/transient-io race, real because MCP clients issue parallel tool calls; fixed with a server-level mutate_lock + run_mutate as THE single mutate entry (36 call sites rewired); read tools stay lock-free by design
finding 5 (doc drift): design doc sec 7.13 still described the R443 blanket undecidable-exclusion that R447 superseded - updated (local artifact), plus the import_facts_smoke header comment

finding 6 (count honesty): prior entries' 795/797 test figures were an awk summation artifact; canonical method (sum of passed across all test-result lines) gives 792 at the R447 HEAD, 793 after this review (+1 core unit test); frozen entries stay as written, the corrected method is recorded here
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 793 passed 0 failed (counted by the corrected method, HEAD baseline 792); clippy --all-targets 0 warnings; fmt clean; binaries (cli+mcp) reinstalled

live MCP stdio verification (the R446 gap this review closes): tools/list = 50 with add_predicate present; sequential add_frame -> add_entity -> add_predicate -> typed add_fact all green with the typed leg persisted; shape-mismatch burst case rejects through the shared from_exclusive_args message

concurrency probe before/after: a 5-call concurrent mutate burst against one store file produced transient io errors and lost updates pre-fix, and 0 errors with all writes present post-fix (frames/entities/predicate all landed)

Detroit battery re-run post-review: validate-continuity green (26 facts, violations 0); report-payoff-coverage agrees across BOTH order declarations (parallel chains and discourse interleave): uncredited [F-K13 -> F-K05], undecidable []

Round 449 — typed-claim Round B: narrati.decision_summary: Typed-claim Round B built exactly per the R441 frozen design plus the R443 symmetric-skip amendment, on the Detroit pull (R444/R445): the narrative-rules/v1 artifact (consumer vocabulary, never L0; declared per B-1 with the R428 authority-input contract — config-pinned path + optional sha256, the pin check deduped into verify_authority_pin shared with the canon-order loader the moment the second authority artifact appeared) drives two derived violation classes inside scan_continuity. exclusive{predicate, per: subject|object} evaluates per query world over visible typed facts — same frame, equal keyed leg, unequal non-keyed leg (the consistency skip is symmetric on the non-keyed leg, both directions, per R443; each direction carries its own test so build round B inherited the rule, not the bug) — with co-holding decided by WorldCtx::holds_at VERBATIM as its third reader (the R441 binding finding: the half-open successor cut is load-bearing; the gate introduces no interval algebra of its own, and a dedicated test pins gate-view agreement). transition{predicate, allowed} rides the in-frame succession edge only (succession IS the declared adjacency; adjacency over a partial canon order is ill-defined), with unchained same-subject pairs surfaced as a WORLD-scoped cross-world-deduplicated count, never gated — the world scoping via shared visibility catches fork-inherited pairs that raw branch equality silently misses (probe finding, now a regression test). Cross-frame and sibling-world pairs are data by construction; an unknown predicate in a rule fails LOUD at the scan boundary (the R436 typo lesson read-side); rule findings are derivations re-evaluated each scan, never pinned. Opt-in = authoring the file; violations ride the existing continuity severity knob. Config [continuity].rules_path/rules_sha256, CLI --rules override and MCP rules_path mirror both bypass the pin (the R428 --catalog rule). Dogfooded live on the Detroit slice: 6 rules over 26 facts and 8 worlds green under both order declarations; a deliberately strict vocabulary fired on exactly the two real alive-to-dead chains; stripping one succession edge fired the exclusive overlap exactly in w-kara-machine at the offending beat AND surfaced the now-unchained pair — the forgotten-chain-becomes-caught-overlap payoff observed on real branch data. One build observation recorded as carry, not improvised away: the probe-verbatim unchained definition counts transitive pairs of correct long chains.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-validate continuity.rs: narrative-rules/v1 types (NarrativeRulesFile/NarrativeRule/NarrativeRuleSpec exclusive{per: subject|object}+transition{allowed}/ExclusiveKey), load_narrative_rules with optional sha256 pin, verify_authority_pin extracted and shared with load_canon_order (2nd authority artifact = the dedup trigger); ContinuityViolation gains RuleExclusiveOverlap + RuleTransitionInvalid; ContinuityReport gains rules / rule_unordered_pairs / unchained_state_pairs; scan_continuity(store, order, rules) extended in place, all callers updated

exclusive class: per-query-world evaluation (the R441 probe's executable model, frozen), same-frame + equal keyed leg + UNEQUAL non-keyed leg, co-hold search through WorldCtx::holds_at verbatim (third reader — no interval algebra of its own); symmetric non-keyed-leg consistency skip per R443, BOTH per directions, each with its own test; transition class: succession-edge-scoped (succession IS the declared adjacency), untyped/other-subject predecessors outside the rule (partial coverage is the design); unknown predicate in a rule = fail-loud Err at the scan boundary (the R436 write-side-typo lesson applied to the read side)

unchained_state_pairs surfaced count: same-frame same-subject typed pairs visible together in some world yet not directly chained — WORLD-scoped via the shared visibility (the probe finding: raw branch equality silently misses fork-inherited pairs), deduplicated across worlds, never gated

Round 449 — typed-claim Round B: narrati.publishable_decision_summary: Typed-claim Round B built exactly per the R441 frozen design plus the R443 symmetric-skip amendment, on the Detroit pull (R444/R445): the narrative-rules/v1 artifact (consumer vocabulary, never L0; declared per B-1 with the R428 authority-input contract — config-pinned path + optional sha256, the pin check deduped into verify_authority_pin shared with the canon-order loader the moment the second authority artifact appeared) drives two derived violation classes inside scan_continuity. exclusive{predicate, per: subject|object} evaluates per query world over visible typed facts — same frame, equal keyed leg, unequal non-keyed leg (the consistency skip is symmetric on the non-keyed leg, both directions, per R443; each direction carries its own test so build round B inherited the rule, not the bug) — with co-holding decided by WorldCtx::holds_at VERBATIM as its third reader (the R441 binding finding: the half-open successor cut is load-bearing; the gate introduces no interval algebra of its own, and a dedicated test pins gate-view agreement). transition{predicate, allowed} rides the in-frame succession edge only (succession IS the declared adjacency; adjacency over a partial canon order is ill-defined), with unchained same-subject pairs surfaced as a WORLD-scoped cross-world-deduplicated count, never gated — the world scoping via shared visibility catches fork-inherited pairs that raw branch equality silently misses (probe finding, now a regression test). Cross-frame and sibling-world pairs are data by construction; an unknown predicate in a rule fails LOUD at the scan boundary (the R436 typo lesson read-side); rule findings are derivations re-evaluated each scan, never pinned. Opt-in = authoring the file; violations ride the existing continuity severity knob. Config [continuity].rules_path/rules_sha256, CLI --rules override and MCP rules_path mirror both bypass the pin (the R428 --catalog rule). Dogfooded live on the Detroit slice: 6 rules over 26 facts and 8 worlds green under both order declarations; a deliberately strict vocabulary fired on exactly the two real alive-to-dead chains; stripping one succession edge fired the exclusive overlap exactly in w-kara-machine at the offending beat AND surfaced the now-unchained pair — the forgotten-chain-becomes-caught-overlap payoff observed on real branch data. One build observation recorded as carry, not improvised away: the probe-verbatim unchained definition counts transitive pairs of correct long chains.

exclusive class: per-query-world evaluation (the R441 probe's executable model, frozen), same-frame + equal keyed leg + UNEQUAL non-keyed leg, co-hold search through WorldCtx::holds_at verbatim (third reader — no interval algebra of its own); symmetric non-keyed-leg consistency skip per R443, BOTH per directions, each with its own test; transition class: succession-edge-scoped (succession IS the declared adjacency), untyped/other-subject predecessors outside the rule (partial coverage is the design); unknown predicate in a rule = fail-loud Err at the scan boundary (the R436 write-side-typo lesson applied to the read side)

unchained_state_pairs surfaced count: same-frame same-subject typed pairs visible together in some world yet not directly chained — WORLD-scoped via the shared visibility (the probe finding: raw branch equality silently misses fork-inherited pairs), deduplicated across worlds, never gated

Round 450 — session review of R449: padd.decision_summary: The R436/R440/R443/R448 cadence applied to the rules-gate build before anything stacks on it. One real finding, falsified live before fixing: load_narrative_rules validated shape with trimmed compares and the scan boundary checked the predicate registry with .trim(), but the evaluation filtered typed facts by EXACT predicate equality — so a whitespace-padded predicate in a declared rules file passed every check and matched nothing, silently disarming the rule it declared. That is the precise silent-escape class the fail-loud predicate boundary exists to prevent (the R436 write-side-typo lesson), reproduced against the Detroit store: the padded strict vocabulary reported rules=1 violations=0 where unpadded fires 2. The fix follows the one-invariant-one-place doctrine: whitespace normalizes INTO the stored values at load (rule id, predicate, transition legs), and the scan boundary now compares EXACT so the two readers of a rule's predicate (registry check, fact filter) can never again disagree about what the rule names; a padded programmatic rule that bypassed the loader fails loud naming the padded string. Re-verified end-to-end over live MCP stdio in the same window (the R448 verification-gap lesson): the config rules_path path fires, and an override pointing at a padded declaration also fires post-normalization. Second finding: the per-world reporting contract for exclusive violations existed only in prose - now pinned by a test asserting a violating main pair inherited by a fork reports in BOTH worlds, main first (the world is part of the finding; the R441 probe's executable model). Architectural sweep found nothing else to repay: the per-world scaffolding duplication between the exclusive and unchained halves diverges in its inner logic (abstraction would be speculative), and two lenient-parse behaviors are recorded as observations rather than changed — a typo'd top-level key parses to zero rules but the count is surfaced never silent, and per-rule unchained dedup double-counts only when two transition rules name one predicate (probe-verbatim, frozen semantics).
changes_bullets: load_narrative_rules now normalizes whitespace INTO the stored values (rule id, predicate, transition legs) before validation; the scan-boundary predicate check switched from a trimmed compare to EXACT — a padded programmatic rule that skipped the loader fails loud instead of passing a trimmed registry check while the evaluation silently matches nothing

new regression test rules_whitespace_normalizes_at_load_and_padded_programmatic_fails_loud: a padded declaration loads normalized AND fires (pre-fix: rules=1 violations=0, silently disarmed); a padded programmatic rule errs naming the padded predicate

new contract-pin test rule_exclusive_violation_reports_per_world_including_inheriting_fork: a violating main pair inherited by a fork reports in BOTH worlds in main-then-branch order (the world is part of the finding — the R441 probe's executable model, now pinned)
design doc sec 7.12 R450 review note (claudedocs, local)
verification_bullets: finding 1 falsified LIVE before the fix: a rules file with predicate " alive" against the Detroit store reported rules=1 violations=0 where the same deliberately strict vocabulary unpadded fires 2 — the rule was silently disarmed by the loader-trim/evaluation-exact mismatch

live MCP stdio re-verification post-fix (the R448 close-the-gap-in-window cadence): config [continuity].rules_path fires rule_exclusive_overlap end-to-end, and a rules_path override pointing at a PADDED declaration also fires — normalization holds through the MCP surface
workspace suite 809 -> 811 (+2), 0 failed; clippy 0 warnings; all gates green
carry_forward_bullets: recorded observation (lenient-parse trade-off, canon-order precedent): a rules file whose top-level key is typo'd (or whose rules array is empty) lenient-parses to ZERO rules — not silent (the report surfaces the rules count; CI consumers should assert rules > 0 when they expect an armed gate), but a deny-unknown-fields reject is incompatible with the comment-field leniency the artifacts already use

recorded observation (probe-verbatim): two transition rules naming one predicate double-count unchained_state_pairs (the dedup set is per rule); and a fact with Unknown world-visibility does not participate in that world's rule evaluation (it always participates in its home world) — both are the R441 executable model as frozen

Round 451 — Intercept converter spike: i.decision_summary: R444 build order step 3 executed: a scratch converter maps inkle's The Intercept (MIT, 30 narrative knots) onto the declared-artifact contract — section-manifest (beats) + canon-order/v1 (knot-level divert edges) — and the whole R449 stack consumes the generated artifacts UNMODIFIED through the real primitives: import-sections, import-facts (a typed custody arc + the interrogation pair), validate-continuity with rules. The spike validates the CONTRACT, not scale, and it returned four findings. (1) Beat granularity is an adapter DECISION: section = knot, stitches are sub-beat (71 stitch-local diverts deliberately out of scope) — the R445 Finding 5 clause extended from order declarations to section granularity. (2) Computation nodes are not discourse: the first run imported ink function knots as isolated sections, falsified by the store boundary surfacing them as never-named order nodes; the converter now excludes functions (pinion analog: statechart actions vs states). (3) The binding one: knot-level CYCLES are real (two interrogation answers divertable in either order) and the honest mapping is SCC condensation — intra-cycle edges drop, the hub stays mutually unordered, and the live gate surfaced exactly that pair as rule_unordered_pairs = 1, never gated. Inventing an order would have been the B-1 violation; instead converter honesty propagated through to gate honesty on real data. (4) The Intercept's reconvergent flag-carried variation maps to ONE world with zero branch registrations — R445 Finding 4 (carried flags are typed state, not world divergence) confirmed from the opposite direction, a choice-driven medium landing on one world + a DAG order. Negative control: stripping the custody succession fires rule_exclusive_overlap at a converted beat. Branch emission and flag-to-typed-fact extraction were deliberately not exercised; productizing the converter is consumer-pull with the SCE-A1 separate-repo precedent. No production code this round.

Round 452 — session review of R449-R451:.decision_summary: The owner restated the standing standard (textbook structure over cost, lifetime-project bar, North-Star and SSOT fidelity) and the R436-cadence review ran against the R449-R451 window. Verdict first: the architectural spine held — the rules gate is a derivation layer over the one holds_at with no second interval algebra, rule vocabulary never entered L0, the store stayed the single directly-validated SSOT (every entry through the real primitive, every scratch store gitignored), and no finding touches the North-Star axes. Three findings, all repaid in-round. The substantive one: unchained_state_pairs used the probe's direct-edge check, so a CORRECTLY chained 4-step arc permanently reported its 3 transitive pairs as unchained — an honesty surface that emits false signals on correct data is itself dishonest, and R449 had recorded this as a noise-gated revisit trigger; the owner's standard fired it on principle. Falsified live, then fixed by the R443 amendment pattern: design sec 7.12 now pins unchained = no succession PATH between the pair, succession_ancestors walks the single backward pointer chain (cycle-guarded, out-of-band-edit safe), an untyped middle fact still connects its typed endpoints (partial coverage covers the hops; the pair is chained), and the R441 probe carries the same semantics (all probes pass, real arcs at 0). The genuinely-unchained signal survives intact: the Detroit broken-chain probe still surfaces both the overlap violation and the now-truly-unchained pair. Finding 2: the per-world same-frame visible-pair sweep existed twice inside the rule loop — extracted as for_each_world_pair the moment the copy count hit two (the R440 rule), and the two coexisting scoping models are now pinned in prose: recorded edges evaluate per edge in the join world, derived findings sweep per query world, one holds-semantics under both. Finding 3: the two [continuity] pin/path validations were copy-paste twins — check_authority_pin_pair, messages preserved verbatim. Assessed and deliberately left: typed_object_key stays local with its promotion trigger; the ops envelope mirror is the R435 pattern; per-world co-hold cost is scale-gated (measure first). One inherited observation recorded for a future round: committed code cites the gitignored design doc — the store entries carry the decision substance, so the audit trail is self-contained either way.
changes_bullets: unchained_state_pairs semantics: "unchained" = no succession PATH between the pair (was: no direct edge) — succession_ancestors walks the single backward supersedes_in_frame pointer chain (cycle-guarded per the R440 out-of-band boundary doctrine); a chain through an UNTYPED middle fact still connects its typed endpoints (the hops are partial-coverage territory, the pair is not unchained); design sec 7.12 amended + the R441 probe updated to the same semantics (all probes pass, real Dracula arcs now 0)

for_each_world_pair extracted: the per-world same-frame visible-pair sweep both rule surfaces duplicated (worlds loop + WorldCtx + visibility filter + id-ordered pair iteration + cross-frame skip) now has one named home — the R440 2nd-copy dedup rule applied at the moment the copy count hit two

check_authority_pin_pair extracted in mnemosyne-config: the two [continuity] pin/path validations (canon_order_sha256, rules_sha256) were copy-paste twins; error messages preserved verbatim (existing tests assert on them)

scan_continuity doc: the two coexisting scoping models pinned in prose — recorded conflict edges evaluate once per edge in the join world (B-2, the edge is the finding's identity) while derived rule findings sweep every query world (the payoff-coverage shape) — one holds_at under both
verification_bullets: finding falsified LIVE before fixing: a correctly chained 4-step life-status arc (the Dracula S01..S04 shape) reported unchained_state_pairs=3 under the direct-edge definition — an honesty surface emitting false signals on correct data; post-fix the same store reports 0

regression sweep: Detroit green unchanged (0/0), Detroit broken-chain probe still surfaces BOTH signals (1 violation + 1 genuinely unchained pair), Intercept artifacts unchanged (0 violations, rule_unordered=1), R441 probe ALL PASS with real arcs at 0
workspace suite 811 -> 812, 0 failed; clippy 0 warnings; config tests green with messages preserved
carry_forward_bullets: axes verdict recorded: frames never cross-validated (cross-frame pairs tested inert), worlds never merged (sibling tests), order/rules declared never inferred (SCC condensation refuses to invent order), rules vocabulary never entered L0 (registry carries only object_kind shape), derivations re-evaluate / judgments pin, store = single validated SSOT (entries via the real primitive only, scratch stores gitignored per R404)

recorded, not repaid (assessed and left): typed_object_key stays in continuity.rs with its promotion-to-core trigger comment (single reader); ops ContinuityScanReport field-mirror is the R435 envelope pattern; per-world co-hold cost is scale-gated with the LLM-discovery pull (measure first)
