Round 497 — report-fork-tree built: the.decision_summary: Built report-fork-tree (sec 7.21 build step 1, the part with the present pull): the cross-world CHOICE GRAPH the CYOA renderer assumes — every registered world-line with its divergence coordinate (parent + fork point + the branch description = the choice label), the fork point resolved against the PARENT world's composed order via CanonOrder::names (R488 — one node-membership semantics, no parallel fork engine; the R441 binding rule). Reuses fork_chain / the stored Branch (R438/R440); store schema UNCHANGED, pure read projection, deliberately never gated; fail-loud on a fork whose parent is neither main nor registered. Four layers: continuity::fork_tree (+ ForkTreeReport/ForkTreeBranch/ForkTreeEdge), ops::fork_tree_report, CLI report-fork-tree, MCP report_fork_tree. Per-world manuscripts (R466) gave N linear readings; this is the tree that stitches them at the fork points. Detroit dogfood (live): the full 7-world-line graph incl nested child-of-child forks with labels, and ONE honest unplaced fork point — w-kara-shot-todd's fork point det-sn-confront is declared only in the child's own edge set, never in parent w-kara-gun's composed order, so the choice hangs on a scene the parent never reaches (the R445 F5 adapter-declaration property, surfaced not guessed; consistent across base + interleaved orders).
changes_bullets: continuity::fork_tree + ForkTreeReport/ForkTreeBranch/ForkTreeEdge: pure projection of store.branches, the fork point resolved against the PARENT's composed order via CanonOrder::names (R488), fail-loud on an unregistered parent, unplaced_fork_points honesty surface (the R466 idiom)

Round 498 — session review of R496/R497:.decision_summary: Session review of this session's work (R496 facts->prose render-loop design, R497 report-fork-tree build) against the textbook-grade / long-term-correct / SSOT / North-Star bar, with an independent adversarial code-review pass. Verdict: no hacks, no SSOT violation, structurally long-term-correct — report-fork-tree is a pure projection of store.branches reusing CanonOrder::names (R488) and the stored Branch (R438), unplaced_fork_points is the R466 honesty idiom (a redundant index of the at_placed=false cases, single-pass, not a second source), and the R496 layer split sits on the North-Star facts->prose->playable loop with the AI confined to the Layer-B render step. ONE should-fix repaid: the headline nested-fork resolution (at_placed resolving against a NON-main parent's composed order — the R497 Detroit dogfood lead finding) rode only on a manually-run dogfood with no regression test; added fork_tree_resolves_nested_parent_composed_order. TWO doc-precision nits repaid: fork_tree now documents that it guards only the dangling-parent case and delegates cycle/self-fork integrity upstream to fork_chain; ForkTreeBranch.description no longer over-commits 'choice label' to the standalone world. The R497 entry's 'reuses fork_chain' is pipeline-level precise only (fork_tree reads forks_from directly + resolves via CanonOrder::names; fork_chain runs upstream in compose_canon_order) — recorded here since R497 is frozen.
changes_bullets: continuity test fork_tree_resolves_nested_parent_composed_order: the nested child-of-child SUCCESS path (parent = a registered non-main branch), at_placed resolved against the parent's COMPOSED order vs base — same node k-1 placed for parent=route, unplaced for parent=main (parent-specific resolution, the R497 Detroit dogfood lead finding now a regression guard)
fork_tree doc: the fail-loud guards ONLY the dangling-parent case; cycle/self-fork integrity is delegated upstream to fork_chain (not a complete registry validator)
ForkTreeBranch.description doc: no longer over-commits "choice label" to the standalone (forks_from=None) world

Round 499 — facts-first craft blind-test.decision_summary: Designed factsfirst-craft-experiment/v1 (code 0): facts-first render-as-projection vs plain prose-first on the prose-craft ceiling; pinned FF gated-defects = 0, craft preference open (V9), session-separated and sha-pinned.
changes_bullets: factsfirst-craft-experiment/v1 designed (code 0): the CEILING leg that sec 7.18 (R469 A/B) and sec 7.19 (R473 scale-floor) left open. Both prior trials refuted the FLOOR while running the substrate arm PROSE-FIRST; this arm changes the one variable neither touched: render-as-projection (facts authored first, prose generated FROM the gate-checked base, the sec 7.21 Layer B workflow).

Two arms at identical premise / structure / budget / tier: plain (prose-first control, the R469 plain arm verbatim, no store) vs factsfirst (author the gate-checked fact-base first — frames incl ground-truth + a named-belief frame, entities, predicates, facts with canon coords + evidence, succession edges, fork points, branches, narrative-rules — then render prose scene by scene as a per-frame projection: state the focal frame holding facts, withhold cross-frame facts = mystery, the divergence = irony). The graded artifact for BOTH is the PROSE ALONE; the factsfirst store is its tool, graded only via blind re-extraction (the R469 fairness keystone).

PINNED (only outcome, deterministic, R464 V9): the factsfirst prose, blind re-extracted under the R469 recipe, shows gated-class defects D1 + D2 + D4 = 0 — a projection of a gate-checked base cannot contradict it, so a gated finding is a RENDER LEAK that falsifies the sec 7.21 "extraction gap dissolved end to end" claim against the scale-floor counter-evidence (store-consistency != causal coherence). The plain re-extraction gated count is recorded as the common-mode extractor-variance control; a leak-vs-noise audit on the cited scene-id + quote disambiguates a real leak from extractor noise.

OPEN (no pin): craft preference by 3 fresh blind judges, a 5-axis rubric keyed on "told story vs list-like statements of fact" (the wooden-prose worry made measurable) + forced A/B choice. Two hypotheses held in tension and neither pinned: the wooden-prose risk (factsfirst loses preference the way the prose-first loop lost 3-0 at scale-floor) vs the freed-craft hypothesis (factsfirst, relieved of continuity bookkeeping, writes better).

Inheritance: extraction recipe + D1-D4 + <=8-rule derivation = sec 7.18 verbatim; reading copies = the R470 symmetric assembly over each story blind re-extracted store, run through the R480 experiment-harness (assemble / shuffle / verify-seal); the untyped-residue bridge (judge-cited errors no D-metric caught) measures the R476 ceiling per arm. Premise "The Meridian Vane" (1898 snow-cut hilltop observatory, the director dead beneath the refractor, a meridian clock stopped at 3:14, an adjuster kept from two reader-known secrets) deliberately structure-matched to Saltglass so premise difficulty is held constant; setting/cast freshly original; Saltglass / Belvoir / Detroit / Dracula excluded as familiarity-contaminated.

Protocol SSOT = claudedocs/phase1-factsfirst-craft-experiment/craft-manifest.json, sha256 3fe79c6f3ead13e4e99e803dc02e808c1aadec1caaf5e4b0e810bd3d4972e50d, pinned here BEFORE any execution session exists (the R453 tamper-evidence mechanism); sec 7.22 added to the design doc as local scratch, this entry is the committed self-contained record (R452).
verification_bullets: craft-manifest.json validates as well-formed JSON; sha256 computed and pinned in this entry before any execution session (R453).
All cited rounds verified present in the changelog before writing the citations (R453 / R455 / R464 / R466 / R469 / R470 / R476 / R480 / R488 / R495 / R496) — citation hygiene passed.

Design-round discipline held: this lineage designed the facts-first mechanism (sec 7.21) + authored its PoC, so it is barred from authoring or blind-judging the experiment; execution is session-separated.
carry_forward_bullets: Execution runs in SEPARATE clean sessions S1-S11 (contamination guard): author / extractor / judge sessions never receive this manifest and are never told a comparison or hypothesis is in play; the orchestrator extracts their prompts verbatim.

The factsfirst arm hand-renders prose (no built facts->prose renderer); this is the first real authoring run that could PULL render-brief (sec 7.21 step 2, the --frame filter on report-playthrough-manuscript) — the run-log records whether the friction justifies building it.
Any post-pin protocol edit is a tamper-evident amendment against the R499 sha (the R470 precedent); the pin + blinding cover tampering and peeking, not design-stage selection bias.

Open after this (NOT the extraction gap, which facts-first dissolves by construction): prose craft from facts (this experiment answers it) and fact RELEVANCE / completeness (the R476 ceiling — facts-first forces declaration but does not invent which facts matter).

Round 500.decision_summary: Executed factsfirst-craft-experiment/v1 (R499 protocol, code 0): facts-first render-as-projection vs plain prose-first, blind and sha-verified. PRED-1 projection-fidelity HOLDS (factsfirst blind re-extraction D1+D2+D4=0, plain control 0); craft preference 3-0 to PLAIN (9/9 world-lines, prose-quality keystone) — the wooden-prose risk realized, fidelity is not craft.
changes_bullets: Executed the sha-pinned R499 protocol (manifest sha 3fe79c6f…, seal ebb6bd90… verify-seal MATCH at reveal): 6 blind author subagents (3 plain + 3 factsfirst, fresh context, §1 firewall, no manifest/hypothesis/comparison/other-arm), 1 blind extractor, 3 blind judges; orchestrator ran harness shuffle/assemble/verify-seal + the gates and never authored, extracted, or judged.

Both arms met quota (≥24 scenes, 2 forks, ≥3 worlds/endings, ≥2 frames, all 6 setups): A=factsfirst 29 scenes, B=plain 33 scenes; premise "The Meridian Vane" (1898 observatory), R469-Saltglass-matched structure, freshly authored text.

PRED-1 (the ONLY pin) HOLDS: the factsfirst arm's blind re-extraction (R469 recipe) shows D1+D2+D4 = 0; the plain control re-extraction is also 0; no gated-class finding so no leak-vs-noise audit; D3 dangling required-setups = 0 for both in every terminal world. Render-as-projection introduced zero gated continuity defect end to end.

PRED-2 craft preference (no pin, V9): 3 fresh blind judges unanimous for PLAIN — 9/9 world-line forced choices and 3/3 overall, factsfirst 0; prose-quality means factsfirst 3.11 vs plain 4.33 (the keystone), stakes 3.00/4.00, payoff 3.78/4.78, knowledge 4.00/4.78. The wooden-prose risk realized: judges read factsfirst as an annotated-solution/essayistic clue-ledger, plain as a dramatized told-story.

PRED-3 economics: factsfirst about 1.52x total / 1.73x per delivered scene of author tokens (209,282 plain vs 318,483 factsfirst, factsfirst including a one-time interface-discovery slice); fact-authoring vs render about 40-60%, the render step near-mechanical; render-brief = soft pull (full LLM auto-renderer marginal/no, a thin per-scene state/withhold projection brief justified).

PRED-4 residue (judge-derived, not pinned): the R476 commonsense/relevance ceiling stands (real causal friction uncaught by any D-metric in both arms); the manifest hypothesis "factsfirst fewer residue via forced declaration" is NOT supported — factsfirst showed MORE judge-cited causal friction, as over-claims of certainty (the projection stated more than its fact-base licensed).
verification_bullets: verify-seal MATCH (exit 0) against the pre-reveal seal ebb6bd90…; manifest sha256 verified == the R499 pin at execution start; unblind A=factsfirst / B=plain only after the seal matched.

Deterministic legs reproducible under claudedocs/phase1-factsfirst-craft-experiment/run/: A/B re-extraction stores, per-world canon-order projections, assembled reading copies, defect tables, and the 3 judge verdicts (prose/defects/reading/judges tracked; *.atomic.json scratch gitignored).

Reading-copy normalization audited: 0 leaked CHOICE/ENDING/scene-id/scaffold markers, 0 empty bodies, prose preserved (factsfirst 9,421 / plain 10,845 words; ~325 vs ~329 words/scene); per-world honesty surfaces undeclared_adjacencies / unplaced / undecidable = 0 for all six reading copies.

Blinding audited: every author/extractor/judge prompt carried no manifest, hypothesis, comparison, or other-arm; judges ran in isolated subdirectories; the orchestrator's incidental size-based unblind during staging never entered any sub-prompt (the orchestrator may know the mapping; it must only never leak it).

SSOT = claudedocs/phase1-factsfirst-craft-experiment/craft-report.md (full result tables + as-built deviations); all cited rounds (R452/R453/R464/R466/R469/R473/R476/R480/R499) verified present in the changelog before writing.
carry_forward_bullets: Result: facts-first render-as-projection is FIDELITY-clean end to end (pinned, deterministic) but CRAFT-low under the current hand-render (judged) — fidelity is not craft. The floor the prose-first trials (R469/R473) lost is solid; the ceiling under this render method is low (front-loaded telling, not showing). Recorded against sec 7.21 / sec 7.22.

render-brief (sec 7.21 build step 2) has a soft pull (this is the first real authoring run to supply it): a thin per-scene state-these/withhold-these projection brief, NOT a full LLM auto-renderer — which would risk the very projection-fidelity leak this run was built to detect. Build stays deferred, consumer-pulled.

Open next (owner cadence): re-run this protocol on the scale-floor corpus for a distributional craft claim (n=1 premise here, one model family); and/or probe whether a withholding-in-scene render discipline (vs the narratorial-aside the projection encouraged) lifts the factsfirst prose register without surrendering fidelity.

As-built deviations recorded in the report (faithful adaptations, not protocol changes): --sidecar is per-subcommand (runbook showed it pre-subcommand); per-world reading order was projected from the re-extracted store's own forks_from/forks_at graph because report-playthrough-manuscript --order lists every DAG node (--world scopes facts only); identical manifest-authorized heading/marker normalization to both arms.

Round 501 — disclosure (discourse) layer.decision_summary: Designed the disclosure (discourse) layer (code 0): the rhetorical axis selecting which facts surface, when, and how, between the fact base and prose — the orthogonal third axis R500's 3-0 craft loss exposed; rung-1 frame-limit tested before rung-2 plan built.
changes_bullets: Provenance + diagnosis: R500 executed the facts-first craft blind-test and lost preference 3-0 to plain (3 blind judges, 9/9 world-lines; prose-quality means 3.11 vs 4.33), while PRED-1 fidelity HELD — render-as-projection is gate-clean end to end. The precise cause (owner insight 2026-06-13): rendering A FRAME stated ALL that frame holding facts (the omniscient truth frame, the telling-dial stuck at max), so the prose front-loaded the solution. fidelity != craft; the gap is a MISSING axis, not a substrate fault — R500 proved the fact substrate sound and it is NOT redesigned here.

The diagnosis = a missing ORTHOGONAL axis. Three axes, the third absent today: (1) truth — what is true (substrate, sound); (2) belief/frame — who knows what (epistemic; the branch frame engine, R430); (3) disclosure — what the READER is told, when, how (rhetorical/discourse; MISSING). R500 collapsed axis 3 into axis 2 (render-a-frame = expose all that frame facts), but frame is epistemic (who knows) and disclosure is rhetorical (what the reader is told) — orthogonal. Narratology names this: fabula (the full fact set = the fact base) vs syuzhet/discourse (selection, order, focalization, disclosure-rate, mode). sec 7.21 jumped fabula to prose with no discourse layer between.

Orthogonality made precise (the schema must NOT reconflate — that conflation IS the R500 bug): the FOCAL frame of a scene BOUNDS the max exposable (a fact the focal character cannot know is unrenderable in tight third — a frame matter, a different gate); the DISCLOSURE plan selects WITHIN that bound (what of the knowable is surfaced, in what mode, when). So a render = the intersection of focal-frame and disclosure-slice; disclosure decisions are per (fact x telling/reader-channel), NEVER per (fact x character-frame). A close-third detective who KNOWS the killer (frame) can still WITHHOLD it from the reader until the reveal (disclosure) — reader-knowledge is not focal-frame-knowledge.

Unification thesis (absorb, do not duplicate — the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant guard): the disclosure axis GENERALIZES five things already in the codebase. payoff_expectation / pays_off (R442) = disclosure TIMING (a setup is disclosed before its payoff). report-irony-intervals (R455) = a disclosure-STATE divergence (reader told X, a character-frame excludes X). The exposure-PoC three additive primitives (sec 10 Tier-3, fragment-exposure / FromSoftware model, 2026-06-11) map one-to-one: exposure-surface binding = the disclosure EVENT (this scene/object discloses fact F in mode M); exposure-coverage = the disclosure-COVERAGE report; exposure-order = the disclosure first-at coordinate + the premature-leak gate. payoff and irony stay the ONE reader each; the disclosure checks DELEGATE to them, never re-implement.

Data model — the DISCLOSURE PLAN (stored, deterministic, gate-checkable). A named TELLING over the fact base (telling:mystery, telling:dark-souls) = a POLICY (default mode, default = withhold — the sparse-frame ethos already named on the discourse axis for the R442 unmarked default: never serialized when default) + sparse per-fact OVERRIDES { fact -> { mode: state | hint | imply | withhold, first_at: the discourse coordinate where the reader first learns it (distinct from the fact canon coordinate = when it is TRUE), surface: the optional scene/object the disclosure rides on } }. MULTIPLE plans over one base = many tellings: disclosure is the DIAL that turns one fact base into Dark-Souls-fragment / classic-mystery / expository-thriller (the North Star "one substrate -> many tellings," now concrete).

Determinism boundary (AI never in the gate — R484/R485 holds by construction). STORED + deterministic: the plan (mode + first_at + surface). RENDER-TIME craft (LLM Layer B, not stored, not gated): HOW to realize mode=imply (which object, which phrasing — "imply via the stopped clock"), and the prose itself (blind-judge graded, never self-graded). The GATE = premature-leak, checked by BLIND RE-EXTRACTION (the exact R500 technique): re-extract the prose, assert no fact whose mode is in {withhold, hint, imply} appears as a STATED fact before its first_at — deterministic over re-extracted facts vs the plan, so the AI stays out of the gate. Disclosure-order delegates to payoff (R442); disclosure-coverage (disclosed / hidden-by-design / never-planned) is surface-not-gate (the R442 dangling-is-a-todo discipline; the THIRD coverage instance after R389/R390 spec + R442 payoff).

render-brief becomes the CARRIER (sec 7.21 step 2, previously just a --frame filter). Per scene it now carries the per-fact disclosure decisions { state F1, F2; hint F3; imply F4 via the clock; withhold F5, F6 }. R500 found the render step "near-mechanical given only the holding-fact list" — the disclosure plan is precisely the craft-bearing input that bare list LACKED: the LLM is no longer told "recite these facts" (which produced the wooden told-prose) but given a show-don't-tell instruction set. This is the concrete mechanism by which the disclosure layer is expected to close the R500 craft gap.

The ladder + YAGNI sequencing (cheapest test first; build new substrate only on demonstrated need — the R499/R500 test-before-build discipline). RUNG 1 (ZERO new substrate): render from a LIMITED focal frame (close-third on a character, not the omniscient truth frame R500 used); auto-withholds what the focal character does not know; this is the render-brief --frame filter ALREADY designed (sec 7.21 step 2, YAGNI-deferred) used with a non-omniscient frame — the single biggest lever, since R500 max-exposure omniscient render is the worst case. RUNG 2 (the genuinely new axis): the explicit per-fact disclosure plan above; build GATED on rung-1 craft re-run showing frame-limitation INSUFFICIENT. Reconciliation with the exposure PoC: "player as a sparse frame" was the closest existing primitive, but its two recorded GAPS — A1 (no primitive records "fragment X exposes fact F") and A6 (canon-order is in-world time, not player-discovery order, so "exposed too early" is inexpressible) — are EXACTLY the disclosure axis showing through; the player-frame is a special case (reader-knowledge approximates an in-world frame grown by diegetic exposure), not the general model.
verification_bullets: Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (the R255 citation-hygiene rule): R430 (frame engine), R442 (payoff), R455 (irony), R466 (manuscript), R469 (blind judge), R476 (commonsense ceiling), R484 / R485 (AI-never-in-gate), R495-R499 all present; R500 is the changelog tip. The exposure PoC is sec 10 Tier-3 (2026-06-11, code 0), not a round number, and is cited as such.

Code 0 — design round (the R496/R499 pattern). Build + clippy unchanged; the fact substrate is NOT redesigned (R500 proved render-as-projection fidelity sound — PRED-1 held). Only the missing discourse layer between facts and prose is drawn.

North Star confirmed, not broken: "projection is the interface; one substrate -> many tellings" — the disclosure axis is the dial that was missing to make "many tellings" real, deepening the multi-axis/perspectival model to truth + belief-frame + disclosure. AI-first end-to-end unchanged: the disclosure plan is authored + gate-checked like any fact, the render is the AI Layer B, the gate stays deterministic (blind re-extraction).

Acceptance is DEFINED, not yet pinned (execution is a future owner-triggered round): re-run the R500 craft blind-test WITH the disclosure layer (rung-1 frame-limited render FIRST), graded by the blind, session-separated judge — the craft gap closing is the success signal. The R500 contamination bound carries: this lineage designed the mechanism and may NOT author or blind-judge its own prose (the R469 discipline).
carry_forward_bullets: Build is pull-gated and per-half (R496/R499): rung-1 = the render-brief --frame filter (sec 7.21 step 2, already YAGNI-deferred) used with a non-omniscient focal frame; rung-2 = the explicit disclosure plan, built ONLY if rung-1 craft re-run shows frame-limitation insufficient. Do not build either speculatively.

Absorb-don't-duplicate guard (CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant): payoff (R442) and irony (R455) remain the single reader each; disclosure-order and disclosure-state checks DELEGATE to them. The exposure-PoC three additive primitives (surface-binding / coverage / order) are the disclosure plan storage / coverage report / premature-leak gate — build them as ONE axis, not five features, when pulled.

Round 501 — disclosure (discourse) layer.publishable_decision_summary: Designed the disclosure (discourse) layer (code 0): the rhetorical axis selecting which facts surface, when, and how, between the fact base and prose — the orthogonal third axis R500's 3-0 craft loss exposed; rung-1 frame-limit tested before rung-2 plan built.
publishable_changes_bullets: Provenance + diagnosis: R500 executed the facts-first craft blind-test and lost preference 3-0 to plain (3 blind judges, 9/9 world-lines; prose-quality means 3.11 vs 4.33), while PRED-1 fidelity HELD — render-as-projection is gate-clean end to end. The precise cause (owner insight 2026-06-13): rendering A FRAME stated ALL that frame holding facts (the omniscient truth frame, the telling-dial stuck at max), so the prose front-loaded the solution. fidelity != craft; the gap is a MISSING axis, not a substrate fault — R500 proved the fact substrate sound and it is NOT redesigned here.

The diagnosis = a missing ORTHOGONAL axis. Three axes, the third absent today: (1) truth — what is true (substrate, sound); (2) belief/frame — who knows what (epistemic; the branch frame engine, R430); (3) disclosure — what the READER is told, when, how (rhetorical/discourse; MISSING). R500 collapsed axis 3 into axis 2 (render-a-frame = expose all that frame facts), but frame is epistemic (who knows) and disclosure is rhetorical (what the reader is told) — orthogonal. Narratology names this: fabula (the full fact set = the fact base) vs syuzhet/discourse (selection, order, focalization, disclosure-rate, mode). sec 7.21 jumped fabula to prose with no discourse layer between.

Orthogonality made precise (the schema must NOT reconflate — that conflation IS the R500 bug): the FOCAL frame of a scene BOUNDS the max exposable (a fact the focal character cannot know is unrenderable in tight third — a frame matter, a different gate); the DISCLOSURE plan selects WITHIN that bound (what of the knowable is surfaced, in what mode, when). So a render = the intersection of focal-frame and disclosure-slice; disclosure decisions are per (fact x telling/reader-channel), NEVER per (fact x character-frame). A close-third detective who KNOWS the killer (frame) can still WITHHOLD it from the reader until the reveal (disclosure) — reader-knowledge is not focal-frame-knowledge.

Unification thesis (absorb, do not duplicate — the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant guard): the disclosure axis GENERALIZES five things already in the codebase. payoff_expectation / pays_off (R442) = disclosure TIMING (a setup is disclosed before its payoff). report-irony-intervals (R455) = a disclosure-STATE divergence (reader told X, a character-frame excludes X). The exposure-PoC three additive primitives (sec 10 Tier-3, fragment-exposure / FromSoftware model, 2026-06-11) map one-to-one: exposure-surface binding = the disclosure EVENT (this scene/object discloses fact F in mode M); exposure-coverage = the disclosure-COVERAGE report; exposure-order = the disclosure first-at coordinate + the premature-leak gate. payoff and irony stay the ONE reader each; the disclosure checks DELEGATE to them, never re-implement.

Data model — the DISCLOSURE PLAN (stored, deterministic, gate-checkable). A named TELLING over the fact base (telling:mystery, telling:dark-souls) = a POLICY (default mode, default = withhold — the sparse-frame ethos already named on the discourse axis for the R442 unmarked default: never serialized when default) + sparse per-fact OVERRIDES { fact -> { mode: state | hint | imply | withhold, first_at: the discourse coordinate where the reader first learns it (distinct from the fact canon coordinate = when it is TRUE), surface: the optional scene/object the disclosure rides on } }. MULTIPLE plans over one base = many tellings: disclosure is the DIAL that turns one fact base into Dark-Souls-fragment / classic-mystery / expository-thriller (the North Star "one substrate -> many tellings," now concrete).

Determinism boundary (AI never in the gate — R484/R485 holds by construction). STORED + deterministic: the plan (mode + first_at + surface). RENDER-TIME craft (LLM Layer B, not stored, not gated): HOW to realize mode=imply (which object, which phrasing — "imply via the stopped clock"), and the prose itself (blind-judge graded, never self-graded). The GATE = premature-leak, checked by BLIND RE-EXTRACTION (the exact R500 technique): re-extract the prose, assert no fact whose mode is in {withhold, hint, imply} appears as a STATED fact before its first_at — deterministic over re-extracted facts vs the plan, so the AI stays out of the gate. Disclosure-order delegates to payoff (R442); disclosure-coverage (disclosed / hidden-by-design / never-planned) is surface-not-gate (the R442 dangling-is-a-todo discipline; the THIRD coverage instance after R389/R390 spec + R442 payoff).

render-brief becomes the CARRIER (sec 7.21 step 2, previously just a --frame filter). Per scene it now carries the per-fact disclosure decisions { state F1, F2; hint F3; imply F4 via the clock; withhold F5, F6 }. R500 found the render step "near-mechanical given only the holding-fact list" — the disclosure plan is precisely the craft-bearing input that bare list LACKED: the LLM is no longer told "recite these facts" (which produced the wooden told-prose) but given a show-don't-tell instruction set. This is the concrete mechanism by which the disclosure layer is expected to close the R500 craft gap.

The ladder + YAGNI sequencing (cheapest test first; build new substrate only on demonstrated need — the R499/R500 test-before-build discipline). RUNG 1 (ZERO new substrate): render from a LIMITED focal frame (close-third on a character, not the omniscient truth frame R500 used); auto-withholds what the focal character does not know; this is the render-brief --frame filter ALREADY designed (sec 7.21 step 2, YAGNI-deferred) used with a non-omniscient frame — the single biggest lever, since R500 max-exposure omniscient render is the worst case. RUNG 2 (the genuinely new axis): the explicit per-fact disclosure plan above; build GATED on rung-1 craft re-run showing frame-limitation INSUFFICIENT. Reconciliation with the exposure PoC: "player as a sparse frame" was the closest existing primitive, but its two recorded GAPS — A1 (no primitive records "fragment X exposes fact F") and A6 (canon-order is in-world time, not player-discovery order, so "exposed too early" is inexpressible) — are EXACTLY the disclosure axis showing through; the player-frame is a special case (reader-knowledge approximates an in-world frame grown by diegetic exposure), not the general model.
publishable_verification_bullets: Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (the R255 citation-hygiene rule): R430 (frame engine), R442 (payoff), R455 (irony), R466 (manuscript), R469 (blind judge), R476 (commonsense ceiling), R484 / R485 (AI-never-in-gate), R495-R499 all present; R500 is the changelog tip. The exposure PoC is sec 10 Tier-3 (2026-06-11, code 0), not a round number, and is cited as such.

Code 0 — design round (the R496/R499 pattern). Build + clippy unchanged; the fact substrate is NOT redesigned (R500 proved render-as-projection fidelity sound — PRED-1 held). Only the missing discourse layer between facts and prose is drawn.

North Star confirmed, not broken: "projection is the interface; one substrate -> many tellings" — the disclosure axis is the dial that was missing to make "many tellings" real, deepening the multi-axis/perspectival model to truth + belief-frame + disclosure. AI-first end-to-end unchanged: the disclosure plan is authored + gate-checked like any fact, the render is the AI Layer B, the gate stays deterministic (blind re-extraction).

Acceptance is DEFINED, not yet pinned (execution is a future owner-triggered round): re-run the R500 craft blind-test WITH the disclosure layer (rung-1 frame-limited render FIRST), graded by the blind, session-separated judge — the craft gap closing is the success signal. The R500 contamination bound carries: this lineage designed the mechanism and may NOT author or blind-judge its own prose (the R469 discipline).
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Build is pull-gated and per-half (R496/R499): rung-1 = the render-brief --frame filter (sec 7.21 step 2, already YAGNI-deferred) used with a non-omniscient focal frame; rung-2 = the explicit disclosure plan, built ONLY if rung-1 craft re-run shows frame-limitation insufficient. Do not build either speculatively.

Absorb-don't-duplicate guard (CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant): payoff (R442) and irony (R455) remain the single reader each; disclosure-order and disclosure-state checks DELEGATE to them. The exposure-PoC three additive primitives (surface-binding / coverage / order) are the disclosure plan storage / coverage report / premature-leak gate — build them as ONE axis, not five features, when pulled.

Round 502 — session review of R501: the.decision_summary: Sharpened R501's determinism boundary (code 0): the disclosure premature-leak gate is mode-INDEPENDENT (every planned fact non-extractable before its first_at, payoff a special case); the gate checks only the exposed/withheld binary + first_at timing, while state/hint/imply is craft (blind-judged) — the half-enforced-invariant the R501 wording conceded.
changes_bullets: Finding (session review of R501, this lineage's own entry): R501 bullet 6 wrote the disclosure premature-leak gate as "no fact whose mode is in {withhold, hint, imply} appears as a STATED fact before its first_at" — two defects. (a) The mode enumeration is wrong: premature-leak is mode-INDEPENDENT — a mode=state fact also must not leak before ITS first_at, and withhold is simply first_at = never. (b) It implies the gate distinguishes state/hint/imply, but blind RE-EXTRACTION yields only a BINARY signal (extractable / not): a successful HINT is meant to let the reader infer the fact, so a good extractor surfaces it — indistinguishable from a statement. The gate therefore CANNOT grade state-vs-hint. As written that is the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant anti-pattern (a gate claiming to enforce four modes while actually enforcing a binary = no invariant + silent broken state), and it blurs exactly the determinism boundary the R483-R488 drift arc and R484/R485 (AI never in the gate) make this project's keystone.

The corrected boundary (the design DIRECTION of R501 is unchanged; only the gate wording is sharpened). DETERMINISTICALLY GATE-CHECKABLE (re-extraction is the technique; the gate is deterministic over the extracted fact set): (1) WITHHOLD — a withhold-mode fact must NEVER be re-extractable in this telling (the binary); (2) first_at TIMING — EVERY planned fact F must not be re-extractable before its first_at coordinate (mode-independent; payoff R442 is the special case setup.first_at precedes payoff). CRAFT, never gated (blind judge): the state / hint / imply gradation AT or after first_at — HOW a disclosed fact is conveyed. The four modes are an AUTHORING vocabulary (the render-brief instruction set, the craft-bearing input R500 lacked); only the exposed/withheld binary + the timing are ENFORCED. The render skeleton is deterministic, craft fills it — the project's standing division, now stated precisely for disclosure.

Methodology precision: "the R500 technique" names the blind RE-EXTRACTION method (re-extract prose, run a deterministic gate over the extracted facts) that premature-leak REUSES — it does NOT mean premature-leak is already validated. R500's PRED-1 checked CONTRADICTION with the base (D1 + D2 + D4 = exclusivity / succession / etc.), not disclosure TIMING; premature-leak is a NEW gate (non-disclosure-before-first_at), pinned only at the acceptance re-run, never by R500. R501's "the exact R500 technique" must be read as the method, not a proof.

Two R501 under-specifications recorded (defer to build, no silent gap): first_at is per (fact x telling x WORLD-LINE) — reading order differs per branch, consistent with the existing per-world reporting contract (R455 irony, R466 manuscript); and surface (the object/environment a disclosure rides on, "imply via the stopped clock") has no substrate mapping yet (fact vs entity vs section) — a build-time decision, flagged not guessed.

Scope + SSOT discipline: R501's three-axis model, the unification of payoff (R442) + irony (R455) + the exposure-PoC primitives, the rung-1-before-rung-2 ladder, and the disclosure-plan data model all STAND — this entry touches only the gate-boundary wording. Frozen-ledger respected: R501 is NOT mutated; this is an append-only same-session refinement (the R450 / R498 review-round pattern). sec 7.23 of the gitignored design doc is realigned to THIS entry, SSOT-first (ledger corrected before the derived doc, so they cannot drift).
verification_bullets: Finding grounded in the stored R501 changes bullet 6 (read back verbatim from the store) checked against the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant anti-pattern and the R484/R485 AI-never-in-the-gate rule — both cited rounds verified present in the live changelog before writing (the R255 citation-hygiene rule); R442 / R450 / R455 / R466 / R483 / R498 / R500 / R501 likewise verified present.
Code 0 — review/design round. Build + clippy untouched; the fact substrate is not redesigned. validate-workspace clean after the append (T1 orphan=0, T3 reject=0, commit-ledger drift missing=0).

The corrected boundary is strictly TIGHTER on the doctrine: it claims LESS for the deterministic gate (only the exposed/withheld binary + first_at timing) and makes the state/hint/imply craft EXPLICIT — it removes an over-claim and cannot regress AI into the gate.

This is the append-only audit discipline in action: the defect is in this lineage's own just-landed R501, and it is corrected by a NEW entry that records the correction, not by rewriting R501 (frozen-ledger; no direct store edit).
carry_forward_bullets: Acceptance is unchanged in shape but sharpened: the disclosure craft re-run pins TWO separate measurements, not one — the premature-leak gate (withhold never re-extractable + every fact non-extractable before its first_at) as a deterministic pin, and the state/hint/imply craft via the blind session-separated judge. The R500 contamination bound still holds (this lineage may not author or blind-judge).

Build still pull-gated per-rung (R496/R499): rung-1 frame-limit (zero new substrate) first; rung-2 = the explicit disclosure plan + the now precisely-bounded gate (binary + timing only), built ONLY if rung-1's craft re-run shows frame-limitation insufficient.

The disclosure-layer-design memory and sec 7.23 of the gitignored doc are realigned to this boundary. R501 + R502 together are the SSOT for the disclosure layer: R501 = the design direction, R502 = the precise determinism boundary of its gate.

Round 503 — disclosure-craft-experiment/.decision_summary: Designed disclosure-craft-experiment/v1 (code 0, sha-pinned): the acceptance test for the disclosure layer (R501/R502) — does a facts-first render under a disclosure brief close the R500 craft gap? Low-touch (reuses R500 plain + fact-base); pins = fidelity + premature-leak; the result decides the rung-2 build.
changes_bullets: Provenance: R501 designed the disclosure (discourse) layer and R502 sharpened its gate to the determinism boundary; both fixed the acceptance as "re-run the R500 craft blind-test WITH the disclosure layer." This round (R503) DESIGNS that acceptance experiment as a sha-pinned blind protocol — the R453/R469/R499 pattern, the FOURTH use of the sec 7.18/7.19/7.22 experiment family. Code: 0 lines (the R480 experiment-harness + the existing verbs carry every step; disclosure is tested as a render DISCIPLINE before any substrate is built). SSOT = claudedocs/phase1-disclosure-craft-experiment/disclosure-manifest.json, sha256 7550633362cc1206778354f49aa64cff01323a3c23ec8d6bf07329b40db1880c, pinned here BEFORE any execution session exists.

The question + the low-touch reuse: does a facts-first render guided by an explicit DISCLOSURE BRIEF (the R501/R502 axis as a hand-authored render discipline — focal-frame limiting + per-fact state/hint/imply/withhold + first_at timing, ZERO new substrate) CLOSE the R500 craft gap (plain swept preference 3-0; prose-quality means 3.11 vs 4.33)? Designed maximally low-touch: REUSE R500's plain prose (the recorded baseline) + R500's ff.atomic.json fact-base; the ONLY new authoring is the disclosure render over the reused store, which isolates the render variable (same facts, only the render instruction differs).

The two arms + the pinned DISCLOSURE BRIEF (the treatment): plain = the R500 plain arm reused verbatim; disclosure = a facts-first render over the reused store following the brief — default WITHHELD (the telling-dial starts at MINIMUM, the opposite of R500's max), each scene in a named character's close-third (NOT the omniscient ground-truth frame, which is the gate's only), the reader-known secrets shown EARLY but via disclosure SURFACES / objects (show-don't-tell, preserving the dramatic-irony window), the SOLUTION facts WITHHELD until each one's reveal scene (hinted only through the planted objects). rung-1 (frame-limit) and rung-2 (per-fact modes) are exercised TOGETHER because "The Meridian Vane" needs both dramatic irony AND mystery, which a single focal frame cannot express.

Two deterministic PINS (the R502 boundary demonstrated). PIN-1 fidelity = the disclosure prose, blind re-extracted under the R469 recipe, shows D1 + D2 + D4 = 0 (still a faithful projection of the gate-checked base, the R500 carry). PIN-2 premature-leak (the NEW R502 leg) = every WITHHELD solution fact's earliest re-extracted canon coordinate is AT-OR-AFTER its reveal scene; any earlier is a render LEAK and PIN-2 fails. PIN-2 is exactly the R502 gate: the deterministic check is the exposed/withheld binary + first_at timing, mode-INDEPENDENT — the state/hint/imply CRAFT is NOT checked here. PIN-2 targets (the withheld facts + reveal coords) are pre-pinned from the source store before extraction (no target-shifting). PRED-2b (irony delivery via report-irony-intervals) is recorded NOT pinned (show-don't-tell makes the early-secret re-extraction fuzzy = craft).

Craft preference (open, no pin, V9): 3 fresh blind judges, the R500 5-axis rubric keyed on "told story with a voice vs assembled / list-like statements of fact," forced choice disclosure vs plain per matched world-line. A FREE isolation signal needs no extra judging: the report cross-references the disclosure scores against R500's RECORDED facts-first-omniscient scores (prose-quality 3.11) and plain (4.33) — did disclosure move prose-quality above 3.11 toward plain's 4.33? The decision rule (in the manifest): the gap closing + both pins holding VALIDATES the disclosure concept and authorizes the NEXT round to build the rung-2 substrate (stored disclosure plan + render-brief carrier + the deterministic premature-leak gate, sec 7.21 step 2); the gap NOT closing means do NOT build (the ceiling may be model craft, not disclosure). No build is authorized by this manifest.

Contamination + honesty bounds: this lineage designed disclosure (R501/R502) and wrote the pinned brief (the treatment), so it may NOT author the render or blind-judge — the render author, the extractor, and the 3 judges run as blind subagents that never receive the manifest, the hypothesis, or the other arm; the orchestrator runs only the deterministic harness (R480 reused) and never authors/extracts/judges. Recorded bounds: plain is a CROSS-RUN baseline not a same-run twin (a "rigor mode" re-authors a fresh blind plain at +3 sessions); the disclosure arm inherits R500's fact coverage; premise reuse trades the R499 novelty-control for render-isolation (judge-clean via fresh judges); the sha pin + blinding cover tampering/peeking, not the design-stage selection bias of the orchestrator-written brief (R469/R499 carry).
verification_bullets: Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (the R255 citation-hygiene rule): R430 / R442 / R453 / R455 / R466 / R469 / R476 / R480 / R499 / R500 / R501 / R502 all present; R502 is the changelog tip. The manifest sha256 (7550633362cc1206778354f49aa64cff01323a3c23ec8d6bf07329b40db1880c) was recomputed on the finalized file and pinned in this entry.

Code 0 — protocol-design round (the R499 pattern). Build + clippy untouched; the R480 experiment-harness (shuffle / assemble / verify-seal) + the existing verbs (re-extraction, report-playthrough-manuscript, report-irony-intervals, validate-continuity, report-payoff-coverage) carry every step. No new substrate — disclosure is tested as a render discipline BEFORE any rung-1/rung-2 build.

Artifacts git-tracked under claudedocs/phase1-disclosure-craft-experiment/ (the .gitignore exception mirrors the factsfirst-craft experiment; run/.gitignore keeps scratch *.atomic.json / *.playthrough.json out while the prose, defect tables, reading copies, judge verdicts, and report stay tracked). The manifest is the SSOT; this entry restates its shape (R452 self-containment). validate-workspace clean after the append.

Acceptance is the experiment itself; execution is owner-triggered separate blind sessions (the runbook's single bootstrap prompt spawns the blind subagents). Any post-pin protocol edit is a tamper-evident amendment against this sha (the R470 precedent).
carry_forward_bullets: Execution = a future owner-triggered run; the runbook's single bootstrap prompt spawns the blind subagents. Low-touch by design: reuses R500's plain prose + ff.atomic.json fact-base, so the only new authoring is the disclosure render (~7 blind subagents, render-not-author since the store is pre-built).

The result drives the build decision: gap closes + both pins hold -> build the rung-2 substrate (stored disclosure plan + render-brief carrier + premature-leak gate, sec 7.21 step 2 / R501-R502); gap does not close -> do NOT build, reconsider. The hand-brief friction recorded in the run is the additional rung-2 pull signal.

Recorded variants if the owner wants more rigor or cleaner rung isolation: "rigor mode" re-authors a fresh blind plain arm (removes the cross-run confound, +3 sessions); a pure-mystery premise (reader discovers with the detective, no reader-known secrets) tests rung-1 frame-limiting alone without the irony requirement.

The question + the low-touch reuse: does a facts-first render guided by an explicit DISCLOSURE BRIEF (the R501/R502 axis as a hand-authored render discipline — focal-frame limiting + per-fact state/hint/imply/withhold + first_at timing, ZERO new substrate) CLOSE the R500 craft gap (plain swept preference 3-0; prose-quality means 3.11 vs 4.33)? Designed maximally low-touch: REUSE R500's plain prose (the recorded baseline) + R500's ff.atomic.json fact-base; the ONLY new authoring is the disclosure render over the reused store, which isolates the render variable (same facts, only the render instruction differs).

The two arms + the pinned DISCLOSURE BRIEF (the treatment): plain = the R500 plain arm reused verbatim; disclosure = a facts-first render over the reused store following the brief — default WITHHELD (the telling-dial starts at MINIMUM, the opposite of R500's max), each scene in a named character's close-third (NOT the omniscient ground-truth frame, which is the gate's only), the reader-known secrets shown EARLY but via disclosure SURFACES / objects (show-don't-tell, preserving the dramatic-irony window), the SOLUTION facts WITHHELD until each one's reveal scene (hinted only through the planted objects). rung-1 (frame-limit) and rung-2 (per-fact modes) are exercised TOGETHER because "The Meridian Vane" needs both dramatic irony AND mystery, which a single focal frame cannot express.

Two deterministic PINS (the R502 boundary demonstrated). PIN-1 fidelity = the disclosure prose, blind re-extracted under the R469 recipe, shows D1 + D2 + D4 = 0 (still a faithful projection of the gate-checked base, the R500 carry). PIN-2 premature-leak (the NEW R502 leg) = every WITHHELD solution fact's earliest re-extracted canon coordinate is AT-OR-AFTER its reveal scene; any earlier is a render LEAK and PIN-2 fails. PIN-2 is exactly the R502 gate: the deterministic check is the exposed/withheld binary + first_at timing, mode-INDEPENDENT — the state/hint/imply CRAFT is NOT checked here. PIN-2 targets (the withheld facts + reveal coords) are pre-pinned from the source store before extraction (no target-shifting). PRED-2b (irony delivery via report-irony-intervals) is recorded NOT pinned (show-don't-tell makes the early-secret re-extraction fuzzy = craft).

Craft preference (open, no pin, V9): 3 fresh blind judges, the R500 5-axis rubric keyed on "told story with a voice vs assembled / list-like statements of fact," forced choice disclosure vs plain per matched world-line. A FREE isolation signal needs no extra judging: the report cross-references the disclosure scores against R500's RECORDED facts-first-omniscient scores (prose-quality 3.11) and plain (4.33) — did disclosure move prose-quality above 3.11 toward plain's 4.33? The decision rule (in the manifest): the gap closing + both pins holding VALIDATES the disclosure concept and authorizes the NEXT round to build the rung-2 substrate (stored disclosure plan + render-brief carrier + the deterministic premature-leak gate, sec 7.21 step 2); the gap NOT closing means do NOT build (the ceiling may be model craft, not disclosure). No build is authorized by this manifest.

Contamination + honesty bounds: this lineage designed disclosure (R501/R502) and wrote the pinned brief (the treatment), so it may NOT author the render or blind-judge — the render author, the extractor, and the 3 judges run as blind subagents that never receive the manifest, the hypothesis, or the other arm; the orchestrator runs only the deterministic harness (R480 reused) and never authors/extracts/judges. Recorded bounds: plain is a CROSS-RUN baseline not a same-run twin (a "rigor mode" re-authors a fresh blind plain at +3 sessions); the disclosure arm inherits R500's fact coverage; premise reuse trades the R499 novelty-control for render-isolation (judge-clean via fresh judges); the sha pin + blinding cover tampering/peeking, not the design-stage selection bias of the orchestrator-written brief (R469/R499 carry).

Round 505 — session review of R504: the.decision_summary: Session review of R504: the .clean normalization verified FAIR (bias cleared); R504's 'repaired deterministically' overstates (manual, harness drift = debt). Disclosure won prose-polish but lost overall 2-1 to plain's novelistic warmth. 'Too many facts' REFUTED by data; the real concern is a missing SALIENCE axis + under-used sparse disclosure, plus a render-world-line fidelity gate and emotion-as-belief-frame anchors for rung-2.
changes_bullets: Verification + fairness verdict (this session reviewed the externally-executed R504). R504 + its run/ artifacts are real and uncommitted. The contamination-repair episode was investigated by walking the full .clean diffs: the manual normalization is FAIR — symmetric in kind (same scaffolding categories stripped from both arms: bracket annotations, CHOICE blocks, the all-six-setups answer key, world-line maps), prose-preserving (no scene prose altered; the only added lines are normalized headings), heading-normalization symmetric (A 29/29, B 33/33), and the strip rule is label-INDEPENDENT. The amount asymmetry (plain lost ~100 lines vs disclosure's ~34) is inherent — plain self-annotated heavily. Crucially the cleaning, if anything, was GENEROUS to plain (it removed exactly the told/list-like annotations that depress the prose-quality keystone), and disclosure still won that axis 5.00 vs 4.22 — so the bias concern is CLEARED.

R504 accuracy correction (append-only, the R502 pattern; R504 itself is NOT mutated, frozen-ledger). R504's "harness drift recorded and repaired to R500 parity / repaired deterministically" OVERSTATES: git diff shows ZERO changes to crates/ or tools/, and assemble.rs still strips scaffolding unchanged. The "repair" was a MANUAL per-run production of .clean / .filtered files, not a code fix; the harness regressions (report-playthrough-manuscript --world no longer pruning, assemble not stripping in the live path) remain code DEBT. The craft result is credible (fairness cleared), but the "deterministic repair" wording must not be relied on as an audit fact.

Craft diagnosis (why plain still won overall 2-1 despite the keystone reversal). Disclosure WON prose-polish (keystone 5.00 vs 4.22; sustained close-third voice, never lectures the reader, flawless surface), but plain won the overall forced choice 2-1 because the whole-story judgment rewards NOVELISTIC RICHNESS — character, dialogue, emotional and thematic depth ("Pike keeps the forged wire because it is the only place the old man still is"), and a tighter forensic knot. Judge 3: "A reads like the finest possible outline executed as prose; B reads like a story someone is telling you." Per-structure pattern: disclosure WINS the withhold-structured ending (BURN — not-knowing is the point) and LOSES the reveal-structured endings (where withholding makes the climax a quieter retread). So disclosure is not universally better; the dial must match each world-line's dramatic intent.

Rung-2 design input 1 — render<->world-line FIDELITY gate (a real gap the experiment surfaced). Judge 1 found the disclosure W2 file labeled confront-burn delivered neither a confrontation nor a burn (a quiet-audit ending) — yet PIN-1 (contradiction) and PIN-2 (premature-leak) BOTH passed it, because the prose was a VALID-but-WRONG world-line. No gate checks "the rendered prose's world-line == the assigned world-line." Fix: (a) cheap — the render-brief pins the EXACT scene sequence for the world-line (report-playthrough-manuscript output), so the author cannot drift the path; (b) gate (if drift recurs) — re-extract -> resolve the prose's fork choices -> assert == the assigned branch, reusing report-fork-tree + world-line resolution, deterministic, AI out of the gate. This is the PROSE analog of R488's FactCanonOffBranch wrong-branch footgun.

Rung-2 design input 2 — emotional depth is CRAFT/brief, not a gate. The clinical-ness traces to a brief that over-specified MECHANICS (what to withhold) and under-specified CRAFT (character, emotion, theme) — the mirror image of R500's "state everything -> wooden". Fix: (a) author character INTERIORITY as belief-frame facts (the emotional WHAT is a perspectival fact: Pike's grief / motive-core), so warmth gets ANCHORED in the base and the render projects it (facts-first applied to emotion — but anchor load-bearing beats only, not a checklist, or it re-cools); (b) the render-brief must carry CRAFT GOALS (warmth, voice, theme), not just disclosure decisions — R500's "near-mechanical given the fact list" IS the cold-prose cause; (c) set the disclosure dial to each world-line's dramatic intent. Honest ceiling: the moving prose itself is model craft (R476) — substrate anchors, brief prompts, blind judge measures; no gate manufactures emotion.

Salience finding (the headline of this review). The hypothesis "too many unnecessary facts caused the problems" was tested against the stored data and REFUTED for this scale: the authored base is LEAN (52 facts / 29 scenes = 1.8/scene); the disclosure prose re-extracts to 2.07 facts/scene, LESS dense than the warmer plain arm's 2.21 (73/33); disclosure was also cleaner (D1+D2+D4=0 vs plain's 1) — R500's facts-first over-claim/bloat is GONE here, not present. So clinical-ness is not driven by fact COUNT. The valid kernel is at GAME scale: facts have NO salience / dramatic-weight axis (all equal weight -> the render cannot prioritize -> load-bearing facts dilute among ambient detail, the R476 relevance ceiling). The disclosure default-withhold SPARSE design is the intended mitigation (only load-bearing facts get an explicit disclosure decision = a de-facto salience filter), but this experiment UNDER-USED it — the brief said "state the focal frame's holding facts" (surface ALL) rather than a sparse selected few. Design input: a salience signal + rigorous sparse-disclosure use so a few load-bearing facts render warmly rather than many flatly.
verification_bullets: Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (R255 hygiene): R442 / R455 / R466 / R476 / R488 / R500 / R502 / R504 all present; R504 is the changelog tip. This entry does NOT mutate R504 (frozen-ledger); it is an append-only review + correction (the R450 / R498 / R502 same-lineage-review pattern).

Code 0 — review round. The fairness verdict and all numbers are from deterministically inspecting the experiment artifacts already in the store tree (run/ prose, the re-extracted A/B stores, the judge verdicts, the .clean diffs); build + clippy untouched; validate-workspace clean after the append.

The salience claim is data-grounded, not asserted: authored ff.atomic.json = 52 facts / 29 scenes (1.8/scene); re-extracted disclosure A = 60/29 (2.07/scene); plain B = 73/33 (2.21/scene) — the warmer arm is the DENSER one, refuting fact-bloat-as-cause; disclosure D1+D2+D4=0 vs plain's 1 confirms no over-claim in the disclosure arm.

No commit / no push — recording only. The R504 framing-correction-vs-recommit decision and the harness code-fix + clean re-judge remain owner-pending (this entry records the findings; it does not action them).
carry_forward_bullets: Two rung-2 build inputs (design requirements for the disclosure-substrate round when pulled): (1) a render<->world-line FIDELITY gate — re-extract -> resolve fork choices -> assert == assigned branch (reuse report-fork-tree + world-line resolution; the prose analog of R488 FactCanonOffBranch) + the render-brief pins the world-line's exact scene sequence; (2) emotional depth via belief-frame emotion anchors (load-bearing beats only) + a craft-bearing render-brief (warmth/voice/theme, not just disclosure decisions) + a structure-aware disclosure dial (withhold for withhold-endings, build-to-reveal for reveal-endings). The moving-prose ceiling stays model craft (R476), blind-judged.

Salience axis: facts carry no dramatic-weight marker -> at game scale, load-bearing facts dilute among ambient detail (R476 relevance ceiling). Mitigation = rigorous default-withhold SPARSE disclosure (only load-bearing facts get an explicit disclosure decision = a de-facto salience filter); the experiment under-used it ("state holding facts" = surface all). A salience signal + sparse-disclosure discipline is the scale design input. NOT a problem at novella scale — this run's base was lean (1.8 facts/scene), so fact count is not what cooled the prose.

Harness drift remains code DEBT (report-playthrough-manuscript --world prune + assemble strip regressed since R500; repaired manually per-run, not in code). Gold-standard next step before any further blind-judge run: fix the harness in code, then re-judge over harness-produced clean copies (render + extraction + the two pins are reusable). R504's craft result is credible (the .clean cleaning was fair), but its "repaired deterministically" wording is not an audit fact.

Rung-2 design input 1 — render<->world-line FIDELITY gate (a real gap the experiment surfaced). Judge 1 found the disclosure W2 file labeled confront-burn delivered neither a confrontation nor a burn (a quiet-audit ending) — yet PIN-1 (contradiction) and PIN-2 (premature-leak) BOTH passed it, because the prose was a VALID-but-WRONG world-line. No gate checks "the rendered prose's world-line == the assigned world-line." Fix: (a) cheap — the render-brief pins the EXACT scene sequence for the world-line (report-playthrough-manuscript output), so the author cannot drift the path; (b) gate (if drift recurs) — re-extract -> resolve the prose's fork choices -> assert == the assigned branch, reusing report-fork-tree + world-line resolution, deterministic, AI out of the gate. This is the PROSE analog of R488's FactCanonOffBranch wrong-branch footgun.

Rung-2 design input 2 — emotional depth is CRAFT/brief, not a gate. The clinical-ness traces to a brief that over-specified MECHANICS (what to withhold) and under-specified CRAFT (character, emotion, theme) — the mirror image of R500's "state everything -> wooden". Fix: (a) author character INTERIORITY as belief-frame facts (the emotional WHAT is a perspectival fact: Pike's grief / motive-core), so warmth gets ANCHORED in the base and the render projects it (facts-first applied to emotion — but anchor load-bearing beats only, not a checklist, or it re-cools); (b) the render-brief must carry CRAFT GOALS (warmth, voice, theme), not just disclosure decisions — R500's "near-mechanical given the fact list" IS the cold-prose cause; (c) set the disclosure dial to each world-line's dramatic intent. Honest ceiling: the moving prose itself is model craft (R476) — substrate anchors, brief prompts, blind judge measures; no gate manufactures emotion.

Salience finding (the headline of this review). The hypothesis "too many unnecessary facts caused the problems" was tested against the stored data and REFUTED for this scale: the authored base is LEAN (52 facts / 29 scenes = 1.8/scene); the disclosure prose re-extracts to 2.07 facts/scene, LESS dense than the warmer plain arm's 2.21 (73/33); disclosure was also cleaner (D1+D2+D4=0 vs plain's 1) — R500's facts-first over-claim/bloat is GONE here, not present. So clinical-ness is not driven by fact COUNT. The valid kernel is at GAME scale: facts have NO salience / dramatic-weight axis (all equal weight -> the render cannot prioritize -> load-bearing facts dilute among ambient detail, the R476 relevance ceiling). The disclosure default-withhold SPARSE design is the intended mitigation (only load-bearing facts get an explicit disclosure decision = a de-facto salience filter), but this experiment UNDER-USED it — the brief said "state the focal frame's holding facts" (surface ALL) rather than a sparse selected few. Design input: a salience signal + rigorous sparse-disclosure use so a few load-bearing facts render warmly rather than many flatly.
publishable_verification_bullets: Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (R255 hygiene): R442 / R455 / R466 / R476 / R488 / R500 / R502 / R504 all present; R504 is the changelog tip. This entry does NOT mutate R504 (frozen-ledger); it is an append-only review + correction (the R450 / R498 / R502 same-lineage-review pattern).

Code 0 — review round. The fairness verdict and all numbers are from deterministically inspecting the experiment artifacts already in the store tree (run/ prose, the re-extracted A/B stores, the judge verdicts, the .clean diffs); build + clippy untouched; validate-workspace clean after the append.

The salience claim is data-grounded, not asserted: authored ff.atomic.json = 52 facts / 29 scenes (1.8/scene); re-extracted disclosure A = 60/29 (2.07/scene); plain B = 73/33 (2.21/scene) — the warmer arm is the DENSER one, refuting fact-bloat-as-cause; disclosure D1+D2+D4=0 vs plain's 1 confirms no over-claim in the disclosure arm.

No commit / no push — recording only. The R504 framing-correction-vs-recommit decision and the harness code-fix + clean re-judge remain owner-pending (this entry records the findings; it does not action them).
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Two rung-2 build inputs (design requirements for the disclosure-substrate round when pulled): (1) a render<->world-line FIDELITY gate — re-extract -> resolve fork choices -> assert == assigned branch (reuse report-fork-tree + world-line resolution; the prose analog of R488 FactCanonOffBranch) + the render-brief pins the world-line's exact scene sequence; (2) emotional depth via belief-frame emotion anchors (load-bearing beats only) + a craft-bearing render-brief (warmth/voice/theme, not just disclosure decisions) + a structure-aware disclosure dial (withhold for withhold-endings, build-to-reveal for reveal-endings). The moving-prose ceiling stays model craft (R476), blind-judged.

Salience axis: facts carry no dramatic-weight marker -> at game scale, load-bearing facts dilute among ambient detail (R476 relevance ceiling). Mitigation = rigorous default-withhold SPARSE disclosure (only load-bearing facts get an explicit disclosure decision = a de-facto salience filter); the experiment under-used it ("state holding facts" = surface all). A salience signal + sparse-disclosure discipline is the scale design input. NOT a problem at novella scale — this run's base was lean (1.8 facts/scene), so fact count is not what cooled the prose.

Harness drift remains code DEBT (report-playthrough-manuscript --world prune + assemble strip regressed since R500; repaired manually per-run, not in code). Gold-standard next step before any further blind-judge run: fix the harness in code, then re-judge over harness-produced clean copies (render + extraction + the two pins are reusable). R504's craft result is credible (the .clean cleaning was fair), but its "repaired deterministically" wording is not an audit fact.

Round 506 — disclosure rung-2 substrate.decision_summary: Designed the disclosure rung-2 substrate build (code 0, the R496/R499 pattern), authorized by R504's validation: a stored disclosure plan (a telling registry, schema v21 -> v22), the --telling render-brief carrier on report-playthrough-manuscript, and two deterministic render-acceptance gates (premature-leak per R502 + a NEW render<->world-line fidelity gate per R505) matched by typed-claim tuple to remove R505's manual-surgery caveat. Salience is EMERGENT from rigorous sparse default-withhold disclosure (not a new axis); emotion is belief-frame anchors + a craft-bearing brief (no new substrate). Build staged + pull-gated; the fact substrate is not redesigned.
changes_bullets: Mandate + scope. R504 validated disclosure-as-render-discipline (prose-quality keystone reversed 5.00 vs 4.22, both deterministic pins held) and authorized building the rung-2 substrate; this round DESIGNS that build (not the build), the R496/R499 code-0 pattern. The fact substrate is NOT redesigned (R500/R504 proved render-as-projection fidelity sound); sec 7.23's data model (R501) + gate boundary (R502) are taken as given and made buildable against the real substrate (NarrativeFact at narrative.rs, CanonOrder + FactCanonOffBranch + the continuity defect taxonomy at continuity.rs, Branch/fork_chain, the add_predicate registry pattern + CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION=21 + the AtomicStore shape at atomic/lib.rs, the report-* ops verbs at ops/lib.rs), folding in the two R505 inputs + the R505 salience finding. Grounded by a direct substrate survey, file:line refs in sec 7.24.

Schema v21 -> v22 (the new-field-default pattern, the v6 -> v7 epub_locator precedent). New top-level registry disclosure_plans: BTreeMap<telling-id, DisclosurePlan>, mirroring frames/branches/entities/predicates (R430/436/437/446). DisclosurePlan { description, default_mode (serde-default Withhold = the sparse-frame ethos, the R442 unmarked-default precedent), overrides: BTreeMap<fact-id, DisclosureOverride> }. DisclosureMode { Withhold | State | Hint | Imply }. DisclosureOverride { mode, first_at: BTreeMap<branch-id, coord>, surface: Option<DisclosureSurface> }. DisclosureSurface { scene: structure-ref, object: Option<entity-id> }.

The two R502 under-specs RESOLVED. first_at is per (fact x telling x WORLD-LINE) = BTreeMap<branch-id, coord> (reading order differs per branch, the R455/R466 per-world contract); coords are structure-section refs in CanonOrder space (the same space as canon_from). surface = a scene (structure ref) + an optional diegetic object (a registered entity = the Dark-Souls item) — reuses existing ref kinds, no new ref space; surface is STORED for the brief but NOT gated (the gate uses mode + first_at only).

Two gate FAMILIES kept distinct (scope-confusion guard). (1) STORE-INTEGRITY (validate-workspace / validate-continuity) gates the authored store, UNCHANGED — disclosure timing is a RENDER property, not a store invariant; the plan is new data the integrity scan ignores beyond ref-existence. (2) RENDER-ACCEPTANCE gates (NEW, run in the facts -> prose loop, NOT in validate-workspace) operate on (authored plan + the blind re-extracted prose) = the R500/R504 method.

Render-acceptance gate A = premature-leak (R502). A withhold-mode fact must NOT be re-extractable at all; a fact with an explicit first_at[W] must not be re-extractable strictly before first_at[W] in W's discourse order, mode-INDEPENDENT (withhold = first_at never). Timing reuses CanonOrder::le; strictly-before-and-comparable = LEAK, incomparable = an unordered honesty surface (the B-1 precedent). Disclosure-order DELEGATES to payoff (R442) where a setup -> payoff pin exists (absorb-don't-duplicate, the half-enforced-invariant guard). Gate scope = the explicit set (withhold + first_at facts) = exactly R504's pre-pinned-withhold-list set.

Render-acceptance gate B = render <-> world-line FIDELITY (R505 input 1, the NEW gate). The R504 footgun: a file labeled confront-burn delivered a quiet-audit ending and BOTH pins passed the valid-but-wrong world-line. Fix: the render-brief PINS world W's exact scene sequence = CanonOrder::linearize(W) (in-world scenes); after render+re-extract, every re-extracted canon_from C must be CanonOrder::names(W, C) (the R488 check, prose side) AND the terminal scene must be CanonOrder::is_maximal(W, .) (ends at W's ending, not another world's). Reuses CanonOrder + fork_chain + report-fork-tree (R497) — the PROSE analog of R488 FactCanonOffBranch.

The determinism KEYSTONE = typed-tuple matching (removes R505's manual-surgery caveat). Plan fact-ids live in the authored store; the re-extraction invents ids; R504 bridged them by HUMAN reading. The build bridges DETERMINISTICALLY: gate-targeted facts (withhold/first_at) MUST carry a typed TypedClaim, enforced fail-loud at set-disclosure (a disclosure decision on an untyped fact is un-gateable); the blind extractor is briefed with the entity+predicate REGISTRIES (vocabulary, NOT facts — the entity nouns are not spoilers, the withheld claim over them is) so typed claims are id-comparable; matching = (subject, predicate, object) tuple equality; unmatched -> an honesty surface, never a silent pass. SOUND for what matches (AI out of the gate); COMPLETENESS still rests on extractor recall (the same render-side-AI boundary R500/R504 always had). The win = the human mapping step is gone, not a perfect extractor.

Read surfaces (absorb-don't-duplicate). Carrier = a --telling flag on report-playthrough-manuscript (NOT a new verb, R501 step 2): per scene emits the disclosure decisions {state/hint/imply/withhold + surface} intersected with the focal --frame (rung-1); the craft-bearing brief R500's bare fact-list lacked. This step ALSO fixes the harness regressions (R505 input 3, same code path): --world re-prunes to in-world (begins>0), assemble re-strips scaffolding -> v2 reading copies are tool-produced, not hand-cleaned. report-disclosure-coverage --telling = SURFACE-NOT-GATE (disclosed / hidden-by-design / never-planned), the R442 dangling-is-a-todo discipline, the THIRD coverage instance (R389/R390 + R442 + this).

Mutate primitives (the add_predicate registry pattern: atomic fn -> CLI dispatch -> MCP tool). add-disclosure-plan (stage_registry_entry, fail-loud dup = the R436 typo guard) + set-disclosure (fail-loud: telling/fact exist, branch registered, coord real, surface object a registered entity, and the gate-enabling invariant: withhold or first_at requires the fact carry a typed claim). Field-invariant parity (CLAUDE.md guard): set-disclosure is the single override write path, but it + add-disclosure-plan share the existence invariants (parity test), and ANY future plan-write path must enforce the identical typed-fact + registered-ref invariants.

Salience (the R505 headline) = EMERGENT from sparse disclosure, NOT a new axis. R505 refuted fact-bloat (lean base 1.8/scene) and located the kernel at game scale: facts carry no dramatic-weight marker -> the render can't prioritize -> load-bearing facts dilute (R476 ceiling). The mitigation R505 named IS the plan: under default-withhold, the facts given an explicit decision ARE the load-bearing few (a de-facto salience filter), surfaced warmly not all flatly. A separate weight field = two axes encoding "what matters" -> drift (half-enforced-invariant) + speculative substrate (no-speculative-build). DEFERRED with a revisit trigger: a non-withhold-default telling needing salience independent of timing, at scale.

Emotion (R505 input 2) = NO new substrate. Interiority = ordinary belief-frame NarrativeFacts (the R430 frame axis), load-bearing beats only (anchor not checklist, or it re-cools), disclosed via the plan; the carrier surfaces the focal frame's belief facts so the render has the material. Warmth/voice/theme = render-brief CRAFT (Layer B, blind-judged, never gated). The structure-aware dial = a brief guideline (withhold for withhold-endings, build-to-reveal for reveal-endings, the R504 pattern). Ceiling = model craft (R476); no gate manufactures emotion.

Build sequencing (YAGNI smallest-first, each step independently pull-gated): (1) schema v22; (2) add-disclosure-plan + set-disclosure; (3) the --telling carrier + harness-regression fix; (4) report-disclosure-coverage; (5) premature-leak gate; (6) fidelity gate. Steps 1-3 = the minimum to re-run the experiment tool-assisted (caveat gone); 5-6 = the gates R504 ran by hand; 4 = the coverage surface.
verification_bullets: Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (R255 hygiene): R389/R390 (coverage), R430 (frame engine), R436/R437/R446 (registries), R442 (payoff), R455 (irony), R466 (manuscript), R469 (blind judge), R476 (commonsense ceiling), R484/R485 (AI-never-in-gate), R488 (FactCanonOffBranch), R497 (report-fork-tree), R500/R501/R502/R503/R504 all present; R505 is the changelog tip. This is a NEW entry (R506), not a mutation of any frozen entry.

Build + clippy untouched; the schema v21 -> v22 bump is DESIGNED, built only when pulled (no actual bump this round); validate-workspace clean after the append. Determinism boundary holds (R484/R485): the AI authors the plan (a deterministic artifact) + renders prose (Layer B craft) + extracts (a render-side artifact); the two render-acceptance gates are deterministic tuple/coordinate comparisons over (plan + re-extraction). The typed-tuple matching is the explicit mechanism that removes R505's HUMAN mapping step — soundness is exact, completeness rests on extractor recall (recorded honestly, the same boundary R500/R504 had).

Absorb-don't-duplicate respected (the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant guard): payoff (R442) and irony (R455) stay the single reader each; disclosure-order delegates to payoff, the coverage surface follows the R442 dangling-is-a-todo discipline; no primitive is re-implemented. The two under-specs R502 flagged (first_at per world-line; surface ref mapping) are resolved with existing ref kinds — no new ref space.
No commit-then-build: this round records the design only (code 0). No push without explicit owner consent (the CLAUDE.md git rule).
carry_forward_bullets: Build is staged + pull-gated (do not build speculatively): (1) schema v21 -> v22 disclosure_plans registry; (2) add-disclosure-plan + set-disclosure; (3) the --telling carrier on report-playthrough-manuscript + the harness --world-prune / assemble-strip regression fix (R505 input 3); (4) report-disclosure-coverage (surface-not-gate); (5) the premature-leak render-acceptance gate; (6) the render <-> world-line fidelity render-acceptance gate. Steps 1-3 = the minimum to re-run the experiment tool-assisted.

Acceptance (the build's decision rule; execution = a future owner round, this lineage may NOT author/judge — R469 contamination bound): re-run disclosure-craft-experiment/v2 with the substrate; (a) the tool-run leak + fidelity gates REPRODUCE R504's manual pin verdicts; (b) a NEGATIVE test — the fidelity gate REJECTS the R504 mislabeled W2 file (proves it catches the wrong-world-line footgun); (c) PIN-1/PIN-2 hold on the tool path; (d) craft re-judged blind (emotion anchor + craft-brief to lift the overall forced choice plain edged 2-1). Build justified by (a)+(b); the craft lift (d) is the North-Star payoff, not a substrate gate (the R476 ceiling).

Deferred (YAGNI + revisit triggers): an explicit per-fact salience/weight field — only if a non-withhold-default telling needs salience independent of disclosure timing at scale; surface-per-world, clear-disclosure, plan-edit-beyond-set — thin follow-ons on demonstrated authoring friction. The extractor stays a render-side AI; the vocabulary-sharing harness contract (entity+predicate registries, not facts) is recorded with its contamination bound.
