Round 521 — top-down authoring + structu.decision_summary: From the post-R520 owner dialogue. R520 proved AI-self-authoring reachable at small scale but surfaced 2 blemishes the gates did not catch (a late-reveal harbourmaster sc-07e; a cross-branch prose allusion sc-07a), and R520 grouped both as the R476 ceiling. The owner punctured that on both counts, and this code-0 design round formalizes the corrections as the authoring discipline the SCALE axis requires. Honest refinement (append-only, R520 NOT mutated, the R502/R505 pattern): only case-1 (a MISSING setup) is the true ceiling — you cannot deterministically detect the absence of a fact whose necessity is a judgment; case-2 (a reference to a fact living only in a sibling world-line) is exactly the cross-branch reachability that R488 FactCanonOffBranch already decides for canon_from, and it slipped only because the allusion was free prose (no structural edge) and because evidence references are existence-checked but NOT reachability-checked (verified in code: continuity.rs:1165 existence-only vs the le() reachability at :126 applied to canon_from at the FactCanonOffBranch check). Piece A = the top-down authoring contract: redesign the bottom-up author-brief into phased top-down (scope + skeleton tentpoles first — each world-line's ending, the load-bearing entities, the fork, the major reveals AND their setups together — then detail fill); the substrate already supports any authoring order (facts placed by canon_from), so it is a CONTRACT change not a build; it seeds case-1 (the proof mechanism becomes a tentpole) and makes salience EMERGENT (the skeleton IS the load-bearing declaration, a second mechanism for the R505/R506 salience-is-emergent conclusion). Piece B = the structural backreference discipline: a claim may not allude in free prose; a backreference must cite the establishing scene in evidence, and the one small buildable lever is extending the R488 le() reachability check from canon_from to evidence (deterministic, AI-out-of-gate, same kind as FactCanonOffBranch — spine evidence passes, sibling-branch evidence fails), which catches case-2; built WITH the scale experiment per YAGNI, not now. Combined they fix both blemishes with no new substrate. This is the SCALE-axis input: the scale experiment authors at 60+ scenes under this contract, with the two known failure modes mitigated by construction so a coherence failure at scale would be a deeper finding.
changes_bullets: Took the post-R520 owner dialogue and formalized two authoring-discipline corrections as the SCALE-axis input (code 0, R496/R499 pattern), honestly refining R520's both-blemishes-are-the-R476-ceiling: only case-1 (a missing setup) is the ceiling; case-2 (a cross-branch reference) is catchable (append-only refinement, R520 not mutated, the R502/R505 pattern).

Grounded case-2 in code: the R488 le() cross-branch reachability (continuity.rs:126) is applied to canon_from (FactCanonOffBranch) but NOT to evidence (existence-checked only, continuity.rs:1165), so a structural backreference to a sibling-branch scene passes today — case-2 is a contract + small-gate gap, not the ceiling.

Piece A — the top-down authoring contract: redesign the bottom-up author-brief into phased top-down (Phase 0 scope + skeleton tentpoles = each world-line's ending + load-bearing entities + the fork + the major reveals AND their setups together; Phase 1 detail fill); the substrate already supports any authoring order (canon_from placement) so it is a contract change not a build; it seeds case-1 and makes salience EMERGENT (the skeleton is the load-bearing declaration = a second mechanism for the R505/R506 salience-is-emergent conclusion).

Piece B — the structural backreference discipline + the one buildable gate: a claim must cite an alluded event structurally in evidence (no free-prose allusion); the small lever is extending the R488 le() reachability from canon_from to evidence (deterministic, AI-out-of-gate, same kind as FactCanonOffBranch — spine evidence passes, sibling-branch fails), catching case-2; built WITH the scale experiment per YAGNI, not speculatively now.

Round 522 — evidence-reachability gate (.decision_summary: R521 designed Piece B (the evidence-reachability gate extension) and YAGNI-deferred the build to the scale experiment's need; the SCALE experiment is now in flight, so the gate builds here. It is the minimal, principle-proven lever R521 specified: the R488 cross-branch reachability that FactCanonOffBranch already decides for canon_from, extended to evidence via the SAME le(branch,a,b). A claim's structural backreference (an evidence citation to an establishing scene) must be reachable AND prior in the fact's own world-line; a citation to a scene that lives only on a sibling branch — R520's case-2 defect (sc-07a) — fails, as does a forward reference. Deterministic, AI-out-of-gate, same KIND as FactCanonOffBranch (not new in kind). The CONTRACT half (no free-prose allusion; allude only via evidence) is the R523 author-brief's job; this round builds the gate that makes the contract enforceable.
changes_bullets: New `ContinuityViolation::EvidenceUnreachable { fact, branch, evidence, canon_from }` in mnemosyne-validate::continuity — the R488 off-branch reachability principle, applied to `canon_from` by `FactCanonOffBranch`, now extended to `evidence`.

scan_continuity: after the FactCanonOffBranch loop, each fact whose `canon_from` is positioned has every positioned `evidence` coordinate checked with the SAME `le(branch, e, canon_from)` — a backreference must be reachable AND prior in the fact's own world-line. Sibling-branch evidence (no path in this branch) and a forward reference (in-branch but after the fact) both fail; spine/prior/self evidence passes.
Orderless tolerance mirrors FactCanonOffBranch: an unpositioned `canon_from` skips the fact whole; an unpositioned `evidence` coordinate is not flagged (the forward-declared mode).

Structural by construction — only `RuleIntervalViolation` is counted interval, so EvidenceUnreachable rides `severity` (reject by default) and counts as structural. No new CLI verb: `validate-continuity` already serializes every `ContinuityViolation`.
This is R521 Piece B's one buildable lever, built now WITH the scale experiment per R521's YAGNI gating (the contract is in force the moment the scale author-brief requires structural backrefs).
verification_bullets: `cargo test --workspace` green (98 `test result: ok`, 0 failed); new acceptance test `evidence_off_branch_caught_spine_evidence_clean` proves a sibling-branch `evidence` citation is caught and spine evidence is clean.
`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.

CLI reinstalled (`cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-cli --force`); `validate-workspace` clean (T1 orphan=0, T3 reject=0, drift=0) — the dogfood store carries no narrative facts so the gate is inert there.

Real-base no-false-positive: the R520 v1 authored base reconstructed from its tracked sections/facts/order JSON → `validate-continuity` reports `violations: 0` with the new gate live (its sc-07a cross-branch allusion was free PROSE, not an evidence edge, so it correctly does not fire — the case-2 fix is the CONTRACT half landing in R523's author-brief, this gate catches it once backrefs are structural).
carry_forward_bullets: The CONTRACT half of Piece B (structural backreference discipline: allude via an `evidence` citation, never free prose) lands in the R523 scale author-brief; this gate then catches any sibling-branch backref deterministically.
The SCALE experiment (R523 manifest + R524 execution) exercises this gate live over a 60+ scene self-authored base under the top-down contract.

Round 523 — ai-authoring-experiment/v2 (.decision_summary: R520's pre-committed rule routed pins-hold+coherent to the SCALE axis; R521 designed the authoring contract it uses (top-down skeleton-first + structural backreference); R522 built the one gate that contract needs (EvidenceUnreachable). This round assembles + sha-pins the scale experiment that puts all three to the test at 60-72 scenes — the rematch above the 60-scene floor where the prose-first authoring loop lost (the scale-floor R473-R479 finding: store-consistency does not equal causal coherence). The premise is fresh and original (Cold Signal at Vantor Pass); the author-brief makes the method TOP-DOWN (tentpole facts first: endings, load-bearing entities, forks, reveals-with-their-setups, before any detail) and requires structural backreferences (evidence citations, never free prose), so R520's two known failure modes (case-1 late-unseeded reveal, case-2 cross-branch allusion) are mitigated by construction — meaning a coherence failure at scale would be a NEW, DEEPER finding rather than a repeat. Two deterministic pins (convergence-at-scale, structural floor) + 3 blind judges on per-world manuscripts; the pre-committed decision rule routes both-pins+coherent to SCALES, both-pins+incoherent to the scale-floor finding reproduced at the authoring end (the completeness-aggregation / salience lever gains measured pull), PIN-A1-fails to scale convergence-friction. The contamination bound holds: this lineage (R518/R521/R522) wrote the manifest + premise + briefs and runs the gates, but authors no fact and judges nothing; execution is a blind author + 3 blind judges in R524.
changes_bullets: Created `claudedocs/phase1-ai-authoring-experiment/v2/`: premise.md, author-brief.md, judge-brief.md, manifest.json, runbook.md, .gitignore — the SCALE rematch of the R520 small-scale baseline.

Premise "Cold Signal at Vantor Pass" (FRESH, original): a snowbound mountain railway-junction with a dawn relief train, an inciting telegraph-record-tampering mystery, factions with divergent false beliefs. Scoped for scale: 60-72 scenes, >=2 forks (a primary fork + a downstream fork-off-a-fork), 4-6 terminal world-lines, >=4 setup->payoffs (>=2 long-range across the fork), >=2 belief frames.

author-brief = the R521 TOP-DOWN contract: Phase 0 SCOPE+SKELETON (author the tentpoles FIRST — each world-line's ending, load-bearing entities, the forks, every major reveal WITH its setup placed together) then Phase 1 DETAIL FILL; plus the STRUCTURAL-backreference discipline (a callback must cite its establishing scene in `evidence`, never allude in free prose). Reuses v1's accurate field-rules + gate-command sections.

manifest pins scaled: PIN-A1 convergence-at-scale now SUBSUMES the R522 `EvidenceUnreachable` check inside validate-continuity structural=0; PIN-A2 structural floor requires >=2 forks / 4-6 worlds / 0 dangling-payoff / 0 off-branch / 0 evidence-unreachable. Decision rule = the scale-floor rematch: both-pins+coherent => SCALES; both-pins+incoherent => "store-consistency != causal coherence" reproduced at scale = a DEEPER finding (case-1/case-2 already mitigated by the contract); PIN-A1-fails => scale convergence-friction is the lever.

Round 523 — ai-authoring-experiment/v2 (.publishable_decision_summary: R520's pre-committed rule routed pins-hold+coherent to the SCALE axis; R521 designed the authoring contract it uses (top-down skeleton-first + structural backreference); R522 built the one gate that contract needs (EvidenceUnreachable). This round assembles + sha-pins the scale experiment that puts all three to the test at 60-72 scenes — the rematch above the 60-scene floor where the prose-first authoring loop lost (the scale-floor R473-R479 finding: store-consistency does not equal causal coherence). The premise is fresh and original (Cold Signal at Vantor Pass); the author-brief makes the method TOP-DOWN (tentpole facts first: endings, load-bearing entities, forks, reveals-with-their-setups, before any detail) and requires structural backreferences (evidence citations, never free prose), so R520's two known failure modes (case-1 late-unseeded reveal, case-2 cross-branch allusion) are mitigated by construction — meaning a coherence failure at scale would be a NEW, DEEPER finding rather than a repeat. Two deterministic pins (convergence-at-scale, structural floor) + 3 blind judges on per-world manuscripts; the pre-committed decision rule routes both-pins+coherent to SCALES, both-pins+incoherent to the scale-floor finding reproduced at the authoring end (the completeness-aggregation / salience lever gains measured pull), PIN-A1-fails to scale convergence-friction. The contamination bound holds: this lineage (R518/R521/R522) wrote the manifest + premise + briefs and runs the gates, but authors no fact and judges nothing; execution is a blind author + 3 blind judges in R524.

Round 524 — ai-authoring-experiment/v2 (.decision_summary: R520 routed pins-hold+coherent to the SCALE axis; R521 designed the top-down + structural-backref authoring contract; R522 built the evidence-reachability gate; R523 sha-pinned the manifest. This round executed it blind at 70 scenes — above the 60-scene scale-floor where the prose-first authoring loop lost (the R473-R479 finding: store-consistency does not equal causal coherence). A blind author, given only the premise and the top-down brief, authored a 70-scene / 85-fact / 7-branch / 5-terminal-world branching fact base through its own write-gate-read-repair loop (a clean Phase-0 skeleton of 24 tentpoles, then 4 Phase-1 iterations). The orchestrator rebuilt the store fresh from the manifests and INDEPENDENTLY verified both pins (PIN-A1 convergence: 0 import errors, validate-continuity 0/0 incl. the R522 EvidenceUnreachable check, every branch placement-clean; PIN-A2 floor: 0 dangling + 6/6 typed-substantiated in all 5 terminals, fork-tree 3 forks / 0 unplaced / 5 terminals, 0 off-branch family). 3 fresh blind judges scored it unanimous 5/5 overall with coherence 5/5, all three independently calling it a coherent authored story and naming the same strength: each ending cashes a different subset of the planted spine facts. The scale-floor store-consistency-not-equal-coherence did NOT reproduce. R522 was field-validated (66 backref facts clean + a deliberate negative control firing evidence_unreachable on a sibling-branch cite), eliminating case-2. The one shared blemish — an unseeded survivor on the act-dig branch — is case-1, the R476 semantic-completeness ceiling, recurring on one of five branches despite top-down (which reduced but did not eliminate it); a completeness dent, not a coherence break. By the pre-committed decision rule: AI-self-authoring SCALES — the North-Star authoring loop is reachable at novella scale.
changes_bullets: Executed the R523 sha-pinned scale manifest BLIND: one blind author (premise.md + author-brief.md only) authored "Cold Signal at Vantor Pass" — 70 scenes / 85 facts / 7 branches / 3 frames — working TOP-DOWN (a clean Phase-0 skeleton of 24 tentpoles: every ending + 6 spine setups + the forks + both belief frames, then 4 Phase-1 write-gate-repair iterations); the orchestrator rebuilt the store fresh from the manifests, ran the pins, assembled 5 terminal manuscripts, spawned 3 blind judges, and applied the pre-committed decision rule (R469 bound held: this lineage authored no fact, judged nothing).

PIN-A1 HELD (orchestrator-verified on a fresh rebuild, independent of the author's store): import 0 errors with 70 sections + 85 facts reproduced; validate-continuity 0 structural / 0 interval (the structural count INCLUDES the R522 EvidenceUnreachable check); every one of the 7 branches unplaced=0 / undecidable=0.

PIN-A2 HELD: 0 dangling + 6/6 typed-substantiated in all 5 TERMINAL worlds; report-fork-tree 3 forks (sc-20 with 3 children, sc-26 and sc-58 with 2 each) / 0 unplaced / 5 terminals all reaching a terminal; 0 timeline gaps; off-branch family all 0 (succession_cross_branch / fact_canon_off_branch / evidence_unreachable).

3 blind judges UNANIMOUS 5/5 overall (coherence 5/5/5, branch 5/5/5, completeness 5/4/4); all three independently "a coherent authored story, not an internally-consistent pile of facts", each naming the same strength unprompted (each of the 5 endings cashes a DIFFERENT subset of the planted spine facts) — the scale-floor "store-consistency != causal coherence" did NOT reproduce at 70 scenes (5x the v1 scale).

R522 FIELD-VALIDATED: 66 backref-bearing facts (43 long-range) resolve clean in the real store, AND the author's deliberate negative control (an expose-recant fact citing a sibling expose-deny-only scene) fired exactly evidence_unreachable = Piece B works non-vacuously; R520's case-2 cross-branch allusion is now structurally prevented (the contract) AND caught (the gate). The lone blemish = act-dig's unseeded survivor (sc-62) = case-1 (the R476 ceiling) recurring on 1 of 5 branches despite top-down (top-down reduced case-1 to the 6 declared setups but did not eliminate it) — a completeness dent, not a logic break.
verification_bullets: Both pins were re-run by the orchestrator over a store REBUILT FRESH from the author's sections.json + facts.json (not the author's store.atomic.json) — every gate verdict recorded verbatim in v2/report.md; the author's claims were verified, not trusted.

Control cross-refs (judges blind): v1 (14 scenes) unanimous 5/5 -> v2 (70 scenes) unanimous 5/5 = coherence held at 5x scale; the scale-floor prose-first loop LOST at ~60 scenes while facts-first top-down WON at 70 = the binding difference is authoring METHOD, not scale.

Tracked evidence = manifest + premise + briefs + runbook + the authored sections/facts/order JSON + author-log.md + the 5 per-world manuscripts + the 3 judge verdicts + report.md; store.atomic.json gitignored (reproducible via import-sections/import-facts).
carry_forward_bullets: DECISION (pre-committed rule): both pins hold AND unanimous 5/5 coherent => premise->self-authored coherence HOLDS above the 60-scene floor => AI-self-authoring SCALES; the North-Star authoring loop is reachable at novella scale. The next frontier is a DIFFERENT axis (convergence-JOIN / salience / pinion-projection), not more authoring-coherence proof.

Residual: case-1 (judge-only semantic completeness = the R476 ceiling) recurred on one branch; the lever against it stays the sec 7.26 completeness-aggregation read-surface, still YAGNI (the structural gates did their job; case-1 is gate-blind by construction).

Round 525 — disclosure-craft-v3 reading.decision_summary: The v3 교과서화 backlog flagged A-2/A-3 (drop the inline-Python reading-copy normalization, regenerate canonical post-R516 copies) as buildable debt bundled with this scale session where the harness is in hand. Closed here textbook: the heading-title normalization the orchestrator did inline at R515 (an SSOT duplication of the fact base's titles) is now a deterministic harness flag, assemble --titles-from <store>, sourcing canonical neutral headings from the one source of truth; R516 already made the scaffolding .clean redundant; and story::parse_heading now accepts the bare ## sc-NN form so the reused compliance render assembles without a manual pre-norm. The fact base authored.atomic.json is tracked (a .gitignore exception — it is a source, no tracked JSON reproduces it), so all six reading copies regenerate byte-identically from tracked inputs with zero inline scripting (the no-silent-fail-scripting discipline: a Rust harness flag, not an inline normalization). Honest effect on the judged artifact: two arm-A copies shed 4 immaterial trailing rules the manual clean had left in (exactly R516's modulo-4), the prose unchanged, so the FORK-1 3-0 warm verdict is untouched. This is a reproducibility/SSOT cleanup; the A-1/A-4 blind-fidelity + 3-blind re-judge stay separately experiment-gated.
changes_bullets: tools/experiment-harness: new `assemble --titles-from <store.json>` sources each scene heading from the fact base's section titles (neutral, arm-independent), folding the R515 inline heading-`.norm` step into the deterministic Rust harness (load_titles parses the store JSON, loud on a missing id); story::parse_heading generalized to accept the BARE `## sc-NN` form (single sc- token, empty title) so a reused render carrying no heading titles assembles without a pre-norm. +2 harness tests.

The six disclosure-craft-v3 reading copies regenerated to canonical R516+R525 form via one deterministic command per (arm x world): report-playthrough-manuscript --world W (full walk = 19/18/16 scenes) -> assemble --titles-from authored.atomic.json. Verified BYTE-IDENTICAL to the committed copies from TRACKED sources (run/warm/story.md=A, run/compliance/story.md=B per label-map, the fact base, meridian-order).

authored.atomic.json (the typed fact base + dc-v2 plan) tracked via a v3/.gitignore exception — it is a SOURCE (no tracked sections.json/facts.json reproduces it; reused from disclosure v2), so the six copies regenerate from tracked inputs alone (the track-the-source side of R404).

Round 526 — precondition back-propagatio.decision_summary: The owner challenged R524's lone blemish — 'top-down should already have known what the branch needed.' The store bears the owner out, and this code-0 design round formalizes the precise failure as a refinement of the R521 top-down contract. The act-dig survivor was KNOWN at the skeleton stage (the Phase-0 tentpole f-071 reads 'the survivor confirms…'); the entity theo was spine-grounded (sc-08); what slipped is a new STATE (Theo alive) that answers a question gt left silent — the spine carries the gang's death only as a vigil (mood) and a town-belief, never as a ground-truth fact, so the reveal contradicts nothing (coherence 5/5) yet resolves an unplanted question. R521's contract modeled the reveal's PAYOFF relationship (f-071 pays off the seeded warning) but had no slot for the new state the reveal INTRODUCES. The refinement (Piece A v2): in Phase 0, for each reveal, decompose every fact it asserts and SEED every new precondition — a new entity gets an establishing beat, a new state/answer gets its question opened earlier as a payoff_expectation:expected setup the reveal pays_off, and gt must take a position (or mark open) any question a belief frame raises. Recognized preconditions, modeled as setup->payoff pairs, ride the EXISTING report-payoff-coverage gate (no new gate), shrinking the R476 ceiling to preconditions the author never recognizes. A new entity-first-appearance gate was REFUTED (the slip was a state not an entity; foreshadowing-necessity is the R476 judgment ceiling). No build; the clause is the verbatim Phase-0 text the next authoring run's author-brief instantiates.
changes_bullets: design-doc sec 7.28 = the Piece A v2 contract clause (precondition back-propagation) — the verbatim Phase-0 text the next top-down author-brief adds.

Honest refinement of R524 (append-only, R524 NOT mutated): the act-dig survivor was NOT a late discovery — the Phase-0 ending tentpole f-071 literally names "the survivor", and the entity theo (Margaret's brother) was spine-grounded at sc-08 (her vigil). What slipped is a NEW STATE ("Theo is alive") that ANSWERS a question the spine left silently open: gt never asserts the gang dead (only f-103 gt vigil + f-111 town-belief), so the survivor contradicts no fact (0 violations, coherence 5/5) but resolves an unplanted question. R521's setup->payoff framing has no slot for an enabling precondition = the leak path.

The rule: in Phase 0, for each reveal tentpole, decompose every asserted fact into (a) a setup it pays off / (b) already-established or reader-derivable / (c) a NEW precondition (a new entity OR a new world-state resolving a question / cutting against the reader's assumption). Every (c) is seeded before the reveal — a new ENTITY gets an establishing beat, a new STATE/ANSWER gets its QUESTION opened earlier as a payoff_expectation:expected setup the reveal pays_off. Frame corollary: if gt is silent on a question a belief frame raises, gt must take a position or mark it open.

The mechanism reuses R442 (no build, no new gate): a recognized precondition is modeled as a setup->payoff pair, so the EXISTING report-payoff-coverage gate backstops it deterministically — recognized preconditions become gate-enforced; the R476 ceiling shrinks to preconditions the author never recognized.

Refuted (the R493 discipline): a NEW "entity first-appearance reachability" gate — REFUTED, because the slip was a STATE not an entity (Theo was grounded) and "which new states need foreshadowing / cut against the reader's assumption" is the R476 commonsense ceiling no deterministic checker captures. Round 527 — convergence-JOIN frontier SC.decision_summary: Post-R524 the owner resolved the scope-fork to the convergence-JOIN axis and supplied a code-grounded investigation of how far the substrate expresses a Crimson-Desert-class open-world scenario. This code-0 scoping round (the R518 pattern: scope a frontier in one round, design/refute in the next) captures that investigation, grounds its central claim in code, and hands the design to the next session. Finding: the substrate is a branching-narrative + worldbuilding-consistency-bible engine — its main-spine / key-branch / Frame-asymmetry / Predicate-state / payoff-consistency layers are the right shape and small-scale-proven, but the open-world essence (free-order subquests = combinatorial explosion, availability gating as a first-class concept, a quest unit, conditional world simulation, and raw scale) has no vocabulary. The convergence-JOIN core, verified in code not asserted: Branch.forks_from is a single immutable parent (narrative.rs:82, 'fork ancestry is a forest by construction'), fork_chain is a linear single-parent walk, and CanonOrder + le() reachability assume that forest — so the model expresses fork (divergence) ONLY; a JOIN needs a node with two-or-more parent world-lines = a DAG/lattice the single forks_from cannot hold, and the owner's free fork-join-fork-join alternation is a general series-parallel lattice = a genuine STRUCTURAL (new-substrate) gap, unlike R521/R526's contract gaps. A naming-collision guard is recorded (the persistence 'convergence-B' index already owns 'convergence'; the narrative world-line JOIN/merge must stay terminologically separate). The next session's DESIGN round answers the five scoped questions in sec 7.29: refute-first, representation, the hard merge SEMANTICS (what holds at a join when the joining lines disagree on state), free alternation, and the JOIN-vs-concurrency distinction (a diamond join of exclusive branches is not the same as concurrent AND-thread free-order subquests). No build, no experiment, no substrate change this round.
changes_bullets: design-doc sec 7.29 = the convergence-JOIN frontier SCOPED — the owner's open-world (Crimson-Desert-class) substrate-expressibility investigation captured as the next round's framing input + the code-grounded structural finding + the scoped design questions; the DESIGN itself is the next session's round (the R518 scope-then-design pattern).

Owner's substrate-expressibility map: WELL-COVERED (main spine, key branches/multi-endings via the Branch forest, faction/knowledge asymmetry via Frame, world-state facts via Predicate, payoff/foreshadowing via R442+gates); MISSING five structural gaps (free-order subquests = combinatorial explosion; quest preconditions/gating not first-class; no quest unit; world state recorded-not-simulated = engine territory; scale 14->70 proven vs hundreds needed).

CODE-GROUNDED finding (verified, not asserted): Branch.forks_from is a SINGLE immutable parent (narrative.rs:82, "forest by construction"); fork_chain walks a linear single-parent ancestry; CanonOrder + le() reachability assume the forest. So the model expresses fork (divergence) ONLY; a JOIN needs a node with >=2 parent world-lines = a DAG/lattice that single forks_from cannot hold. The owner's free fork->join->fork->join = a general series-parallel lattice = a genuine STRUCTURAL gap (new substrate), NOT a contract/prompt gap.

NAMING-COLLISION GUARD recorded: "convergence" already names the persistence/CQRS fork (the (branch_id, entity_id, valid_from) "convergence-B index", narrative.rs:142); the narrative world-line JOIN/merge is a DIFFERENT concept — distinct terms so code + audit trail do not conflate them.

The five scoped design questions handed to the next round: refute-first (is JOIN expressible by existing means?); representation (joins_into/merges_at -> DAG; what of fork_chain/fork_ancestry/CanonOrder/le() survives); the hard SEMANTICS (what holds at a join when the joining lines disagree on state — reconcile-gate vs parameterize vs authored reconciliation, likely a new "no unreconciled state across a join" gate in the R488/R522 family); free alternation (does composed-order+le() generalize to a series-parallel DAG); JOIN-vs-concurrency (a diamond join of EXCLUSIVE branches is distinct from CONCURRENT AND-thread free-order subquests — do NOT conflate; gating/concurrency may be a separate axis).
verification_bullets: The forest-not-lattice finding is grounded in the read code: Branch.forks_from: Option<BranchFork> (narrative.rs:82, doc comment "fork ancestry is a forest by construction"); fork_chain (narrative.rs:104) is a single-parent walk to root; the "convergence-B index" term at narrative.rs:142 is the persistence concept (the collision guard).

Round 528 — convergence-JOIN model DESIG.decision_summary: The convergence-JOIN DESIGN round (the owner said 'start the convergence-JOIN design round'). Answers the five questions sec 7.29/R527 scoped — code 0, refute-new-substrate-first (R493). The headline code finding sharpens every answer: the order ALGEBRA — closure_of's transitive closure, le() reachability, linearize()'s Kahn topo-sort, is_maximal/names — is ALREADY a general DAG machine (continuity.rs:263 states the closure of a DAG topo-sorts identically to it), so the 'forest by construction' constraint does NOT live in the order layer; it lives in exactly three named places: the single-parent Branch.forks_from (narrative.rs:82), the chain-shaped fork_chain/fork_ancestry (narrative.rs:104 / continuity.rs:991), and the single-lineage visibility/join_world (continuity.rs:1019/1042). A world-line JOIN is therefore a BOUNDED generalization of the ANCESTRY layer, not a rewrite of the order algebra. Scoped to the exclusive-OR diamond, the JOIN is a write-once SHARED continuation (a path-dependent prefix plus a suffix authored once), NOT a state-merge; its soundness is the R488/R522 reachability family checked per-incoming-parent ('no unreconciled dependency across a confluence'), with explicit reconciliation expressed by a bounded cross-confluence extension of supersedes_in_frame succession. Concurrency / free-order / availability-gating is a DISTINCT future substrate axis, not folded in. Build YAGNI-deferred behind an authoring run that needs it (the R495/R518/R521 'experiment is the test' discipline). Working term 'confluence' sidesteps both code collisions (join_world lattice-meet; persistence convergence-B index).
changes_bullets: Q1 REFUTE-FIRST — gap CONFIRMED: three existing-means candidates all fail. (a) a within-ONE-branch canon diamond (a->b, a->c, b->d, c->d) is handled by le() but b AND c both occur in EVERY playthrough of that branch = concurrency-within-a-world, not exclusive reconvergence. (b) duplicating the continuation onto both branches yields two DISTINCT facts (NarrativeFact.branch is a single String, narrative.rs:336) = no shared node identity, the fork-tree shows two leaves not a merge, payoff/continuity gates treat them independently = defeats write-once. (c) a third branch forks_from one parent inherits ONLY that one parent. The single immutable forks_from is the hard blocker; the gap is real (matches R527).

Q2 REPRESENTATION — a world-line gains a MULTI-parent merge declaration (working term `confluence`: the BranchFork {branch, at} shape as a SET, not Option-singular). SURVIVES UNCHANGED (the order algebra is already DAG-general): closure_of / le() / comparable / linearize (Kahn) / is_maximal / names. MUST GENERALIZE chain->DAG: fork_chain (single-parent walk -> ancestor-SET collection, the hop-cap stays as the DAG acyclicity guard); fork_ancestry (Vec chain -> transitive ancestor set); CanonOrder::from_declaration's composition loop (already iterates ancestry as a collection, so it generalizes for free once ancestry yields the full set); visibility / Lineage.cut (one cut -> a cut per parent path); join_world (lattice-meet over a DAG). The acyclic invariant is preserved: a confluence may not merge a branch into its own ancestor.

Q3 SEMANTICS (the crux) — SCOPE to the exclusive-OR diamond ==> a JOIN is a PATH-DEPENDENT continuation that SHARES authored facts, NOT a state-merge. Any single playthrough traverses EXACTLY ONE incoming line, so "what holds at the confluence" is W1's-prefix OR W2's-prefix plus the shared suffix authored ONCE. The primitive buys WRITE-ONCE shared continuation (author the ending once, both lines inherit) = directly attacks the forest's duplication tax (all-roads-lead-to-the-ending without authoring it N times). The soundness GATE (the R488/R522 le() analog) = "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence": every fact visible in the shared continuation must have its support (evidence / pays_off targets / referenced entity-state) reachable from EVERY incoming parent via le() checked PER-PARENT; a continuation fact depending on a prefix present on only SOME entries = the false-merge bug = REJECT (deterministic, AI-out-of-gate, the R502 family).

Q3 cont. RECONCILIATION — where content MUST differ by entry, you do NOT converge (stay forked) OR author an explicit reconciliation at the confluence: the EXISTING supersedes_in_frame succession with ONE bounded exception — succession may cross INTO a confluence node from its parents (today SuccessionCrossBranch, continuity.rs:840, rejects ALL cross-branch succession; the confluence is the single principled exception, a successor there supersedes each parent's divergent belief). No state-reconciliation ENGINE and no auto-merge logic (that = the R476 ceiling / world-simulation / pinion territory, deliberately out).

Q4 FREE ALTERNATION — fork->join->fork->join GENERALIZES to a series-parallel DAG, CONDITIONAL on Q2's multi-parent ancestry. Kahn linearize() already topo-sorts any DAG (continuity.rs:263 "the closure of a DAG topo-sorts identically to it"); le() answers cross-lattice reachability once from_declaration composes over the ancestor SET. The ancestor-CHAIN is the ONLY thing replaced. Multi-parent ancestry is MORE general than series-parallel (allows arbitrary acyclic merges); an optional "well-formed diamond" structural surface (every confluence's parents share a fork ancestor; the R442/R488 report-not-gate family) is DEFERRED — do not constrain mechanically up front.

Q5 JOIN vs CONCURRENCY — SEPARATE AXES, do NOT fold in. Convergence-JOIN = the exclusive-OR diamond (one path per playthrough -> tractable as sharing + per-parent reachability, this scope). Concurrency / quest-graph = AND-threads, author-free interleaving, availability GATING, state COMBINED (real conflicts), a quest unit (R527 open-world gaps #1/#2/#3) = the combinatorial explosion; it needs a different model (an independence/concurrency relation — event-structure / Mazurkiewicz-trace shape — plus a gating/precondition layer), its own future R518-style scope round. Folding it in would import the explosion into an otherwise clean bounded generalization.

BUILD DECISION — DESIGN captured, BUILD DEFERRED behind an authoring run that HITS the wall (the R493/R495/R518/R521 doctrine: build only when an experiment shows pull; R524 SCALED to 70 scenes inside the forest with NO merge needed, so there is no pull yet). The next authoring/scale run's brief gains a many-world-lines-converge-to-one-ending requirement; if the forest forces ugly duplication or the author cannot express the shared continuation, THAT failure is the build trigger (exactly how R521's contract -> R524's scale run worked). Naming-guard carried: `confluence` (narrative world-line merge) != join_world (the conflict-scope lattice-meet, continuity.rs:1042) != persistence convergence-B index (narrative.rs:142); the final identifier is a build-time decision.
verification_bullets: Code-grounded, not asserted — the order algebra's DAG-generality READ in continuity.rs: closure_of (transitive closure over arbitrary edges, lines 88-124), le()/comparable (214-221), names/is_maximal (239-257), linearize Kahn (266-296, doc comment line 263). The forest constraint READ at narrative.rs:81-82 (forks_from: Option<BranchFork>), narrative.rs:104-120 (fork_chain single-parent walk), continuity.rs:991-1005 (fork_ancestry -> Vec chain), continuity.rs:1019-1060 (visibility / join_world single-lineage); the cross-branch-succession reject at continuity.rs:838-845 (SuccessionCrossBranch). The conclusion "the forest lives in three named places, not the order layer" is a read finding.

Refute-first applied (the R493 discipline): each Q1 candidate tested against the actual types (NarrativeFact.branch is a single String at narrative.rs:336-337; Branch.forks_from is Option-singular at narrative.rs:81-82) before concluding the gap is real, rather than asserting it from R527.

Naming-collision guard re-verified in code: join_world (continuity.rs:1042) already uses "join" for the conflict-scope lattice-meet; the persistence convergence-B index is narrative.rs:142-143 — both distinct from the narrative world-line merge; the working term `confluence` sidesteps both.

code 0; no mutation of any prior entry (design-doc sec 7.30 + this ledger are append-only over sec 7.29/R527 — the R450/R498/R526 append-only-refinement discipline); validate-workspace clean; all citations (R433/R438/R440/R442/R446/R456/R466/R488/R493/R495/R502/R518/R521/R522/R524/R527) verified present in the changelog before writing (citation hygiene).
carry_forward_bullets: NEXT (build pull-gated, NOT auto-taken): the convergence-JOIN BUILD is designed and waits on PULL — the next authoring/scale run's brief must require a many-world-lines-converge-to-one-ending structure; if the forest forces duplication and the author cannot express a shared continuation, that is the build trigger (then build: the `confluence` multi-parent ancestry + the per-parent "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence" gate + bounded cross-confluence succession). Until a run hits the wall, code 0.

SEPARATE future scope round (do NOT fold into convergence-JOIN): the concurrency / quest-graph axis — AND-thread free-order subquests + availability gating + a quest unit (R527 open-world gaps #1/#2/#3) — needs its own R518-style scoping round (an independence/concurrency model, not the order-algebra generalization).

The next authoring run's brief must ALSO instantiate the R526 sec-7.28 precondition-back-propagation clause (carried from R527 — per reveal, seed every new entity/state precondition as a payoff_expectation:expected setup).
STILL-OPEN (lower): v3 A-1/A-4 (per-world blind fidelity + a 3-blind re-judge, EXPERIMENT-gated).
PUSH GATE: R528 commits locally; do NOT push without the owner's explicit push word.

The primitive buys WRITE-ONCE shared continuation (author the ending once, both lines inherit) = directly attacks the forest's duplication tax (all-roads-lead-to-the-ending without authoring it N times). The soundness GATE (the R488/R522 le() analog) = "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence": every fact visible in the shared continuation must have its support (evidence / pays_off targets / referenced entity-state) reachable from EVERY incoming parent via le() checked PER-PARENT; a continuation fact depending on a prefix present on only SOME entries = the false-merge bug = REJECT (deterministic, AI-out-of-gate, the R502 family).

Q3 cont. RECONCILIATION — where content MUST differ by entry, you do NOT converge (stay forked) OR author an explicit reconciliation at the confluence: the EXISTING supersedes_in_frame succession with ONE bounded exception — succession may cross INTO a confluence node from its parents (today SuccessionCrossBranch, continuity.rs:840, rejects ALL cross-branch succession; the confluence is the single principled exception, a successor there supersedes each parent's divergent belief). No state-reconciliation ENGINE and no auto-merge logic (that = the R476 ceiling / world-simulation / pinion territory, deliberately out).

Q4 FREE ALTERNATION — fork->join->fork->join GENERALIZES to a series-parallel DAG, CONDITIONAL on Q2's multi-parent ancestry. Kahn linearize() already topo-sorts any DAG (continuity.rs:263 "the closure of a DAG topo-sorts identically to it"); le() answers cross-lattice reachability once from_declaration composes over the ancestor SET. The ancestor-CHAIN is the ONLY thing replaced. Multi-parent ancestry is MORE general than series-parallel (allows arbitrary acyclic merges); an optional "well-formed diamond" structural surface (every confluence's parents share a fork ancestor; the R442/R488 report-not-gate family) is DEFERRED — do not constrain mechanically up front.

Q5 JOIN vs CONCURRENCY — SEPARATE AXES, do NOT fold in. Convergence-JOIN = the exclusive-OR diamond (one path per playthrough -> tractable as sharing + per-parent reachability, this scope). Concurrency / quest-graph = AND-threads, author-free interleaving, availability GATING, state COMBINED (real conflicts), a quest unit (R527 open-world gaps #1/#2/#3) = the combinatorial explosion; it needs a different model (an independence/concurrency relation — event-structure / Mazurkiewicz-trace shape — plus a gating/precondition layer), its own future R518-style scope round. Folding it in would import the explosion into an otherwise clean bounded generalization.

BUILD DECISION — DESIGN captured, BUILD DEFERRED behind an authoring run that HITS the wall (the R493/R495/R518/R521 doctrine: build only when an experiment shows pull; R524 SCALED to 70 scenes inside the forest with NO merge needed, so there is no pull yet). The next authoring/scale run's brief gains a many-world-lines-converge-to-one-ending requirement; if the forest forces ugly duplication or the author cannot express the shared continuation, THAT failure is the build trigger (exactly how R521's contract -> R524's scale run worked). Naming-guard carried: `confluence` (narrative world-line merge) != join_world (the conflict-scope lattice-meet, continuity.rs:1042) != persistence convergence-B index (narrative.rs:142); the final identifier is a build-time decision.
publishable_verification_bullets: Code-grounded, not asserted — the order algebra's DAG-generality READ in continuity.rs: closure_of (transitive closure over arbitrary edges, lines 88-124), le()/comparable (214-221), names/is_maximal (239-257), linearize Kahn (266-296, doc comment line 263). The forest constraint READ at narrative.rs:81-82 (forks_from: Option<BranchFork>), narrative.rs:104-120 (fork_chain single-parent walk), continuity.rs:991-1005 (fork_ancestry -> Vec chain), continuity.rs:1019-1060 (visibility / join_world single-lineage); the cross-branch-succession reject at continuity.rs:838-845 (SuccessionCrossBranch). The conclusion "the forest lives in three named places, not the order layer" is a read finding.

Refute-first applied (the R493 discipline): each Q1 candidate tested against the actual types (NarrativeFact.branch is a single String at narrative.rs:336-337; Branch.forks_from is Option-singular at narrative.rs:81-82) before concluding the gap is real, rather than asserting it from R527.

Naming-collision guard re-verified in code: join_world (continuity.rs:1042) already uses "join" for the conflict-scope lattice-meet; the persistence convergence-B index is narrative.rs:142-143 — both distinct from the narrative world-line merge; the working term `confluence` sidesteps both.

publishable_carry_forward_bullets: NEXT (build pull-gated, NOT auto-taken): the convergence-JOIN BUILD is designed and waits on PULL — the next authoring/scale run's brief must require a many-world-lines-converge-to-one-ending structure; if the forest forces duplication and the author cannot express a shared continuation, that is the build trigger (then build: the `confluence` multi-parent ancestry + the per-parent "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence" gate + bounded cross-confluence succession). Until a run hits the wall, code 0.

SEPARATE future scope round (do NOT fold into convergence-JOIN): the concurrency / quest-graph axis — AND-thread free-order subquests + availability gating + a quest unit (R527 open-world gaps #1/#2/#3) — needs its own R518-style scoping round (an independence/concurrency model, not the order-algebra generalization).

Round 529 — open-world production-pipeli.decision_summary: A code-0 capture round recording the owner's post-R528 North-Star dialogue, which zoomed out from the convergence-JOIN sub-question to the whole open-world production pipeline (authoring -> render -> SCXML -> runtime). The dialogue established: (1) narrative progression maps cleanly onto a compilable deterministic state machine, and SCXML already has the exact vocabulary the branch model lacks (cond guards = state-gating, parallel = concurrent AND-threads, transitions = forks, converging transitions = the R528 JOIN); (2) the AI<->code seam is FACTS — the AI authors structured facts (R524), a deterministic codegen emits SCXML (the report-fork-tree / manuscript / linearize precedent), nobody hand-writes code; the real open seam is how much executable behavior is fact-expressible (codegen, drift-free) vs hand-authored SCXML and only verified; (3) warm render reads FACTS, not SCXML — SCXML emits a pointer (scene + world-line), render projects the facts there, matching the R514/R515 facts-only render and the CQRS pointer-projection; (4) production order = facts-first (SSOT) then render + codegen as projections; (5) render is build-time pre-baked per NODE (scene x world-line), not per path — node-not-path keeps it linear/tractable, confluence cuts the bake count, runtime is navigate+display with no runtime LLM = deterministic; (6) the owner CORRECTED the 'N! explosion' framing — free-order is constraints (partial order + independence) not enumerations, the canon-order DAG already works this way; (7) a 3-level taxonomy: L1 structural fork/join (= R528 confluence), L2 state-gated fork/join (= the gating axis, R527 gap #4), L3 free-order AND-threads (= SCXML parallel); L1+L2 are the exclusive-per-playthrough tractable bundle the owner wants, they layer cleanly (structure -> conditions); (8) the story-graph is already visualizable (report-fork-tree, demonstrated live on the detroit 7-world-line store); a visual GUI is a substrate-0 presentation layer, unbuilt + YAGNI-gated; the full visual = pinion; (9) 100-NPC dialogue = a render projection of (personality entity-facts + the NPC's Frame/knowledge + the pointer), bounded as a node matrix, kept knowledge-consistent by the gates = the consistency-bible strength. THROUGH-LINE: every projection/execution layer is proven or cheap; the ONE binding bottleneck across all of them is AI SELF-AUTHORING at scale + breadth + coherence. No build, no decision committed; this is a frontier map plus the recommended next probe.
changes_bullets: EXECUTABLE-NARRATIVE FIT: narrative progression = a compilable deterministic state machine, and SCXML already carries the vocabulary the branch model LACKS — `<transition cond=>` = state-gating, `<parallel>` = concurrent AND-threads, transitions = forks, transitions-into-a-shared-state = the R528 JOIN. SCE (the sibling engine) builds/runs SCXML deterministically; the CQRS frozen-substrate + pointer-projection framing makes "compiled story machine + runtime traversal" consistent with the established architecture.

THE AI<->CODE SEAM = FACTS (resolves the owner's "AI authoring isn't code, how do you connect them"): the AI authors STRUCTURED FACTS via the mutate API (R524: 85 facts / 7 branches = data, not prose, not code); a DETERMINISTIC codegen emits SCXML from those facts — the report-fork-tree / report-playthrough-manuscript / linearize precedent (facts -> artifact, NO AI in the generation step). Nobody hand-writes code. The REAL open seam = how much executable behavior is fact-expressible (codegen, option B, drift-free) vs must be hand-authored SCXML and only VERIFIED (option A, the proven SCE pattern) = the declarative-truth / executable-behavior boundary.

WARM RENDER READS FACTS, NOT SCXML (resolves "must warm render read the SCXML"): SCXML/SCE emits only a POINTER (current scene + world-line); render projects the FACTS at that pointer into warm prose. They meet at the pointer (data), never touch XML — the web router/renderer split. Consistent with the PROVEN R514/R515 warm render (facts-only) AND the memory's CQRS pointer-projection. Decoupled: progression rules and render voice are independently swappable/verifiable.

PRODUCTION ORDER = facts-first: author + gate the fact base (SSOT) -> render (facts->prose) + codegen (structure facts->SCXML) are downstream PROJECTIONS. The R495 facts-first insight, applied as the game-build pipeline.

RENDER IS BUILD-TIME PRE-BAKED PER NODE, not per path: render each reachable (scene x world-line [x telling]) NODE once at build time; paths through the DAG are exponential but NODES are linear (~hundreds for a 70-scene / 7-branch story) — "render the graph, not the playthroughs." Confluence (R528) shares a converged continuation = renders it ONCE (cuts the bake count). Runtime = navigate (SCXML pointer) + display the pre-baked prose; NO runtime LLM = deterministic + QA-able + zero per-play cost (the determinism the owner required).

"N! EXPLOSION" CORRECTED by the owner (honest self-correction): free-order is expressed as CONSTRAINTS (a partial order + independence = no edge), NOT by enumerating orderings; the author freely chooses how constrained (fully ordered / fully free / between), and the valid orderings FOLLOW from the constraints — never materialized. The canon-order DAG (closure_of / le() / linearize-Kahn) ALREADY works exactly this way, so the "ordering" half is solved; the residual is vocabulary, not computation.

3-LEVEL CONCURRENCY TAXONOMY: L1 structural fork/join (author-fixed topology = R528 confluence); L2 state-gated fork/join (which branch/merge is live depends on accumulated state = the gating axis, R527 gap #4 "world state recorded, not simulated"); L3 free-order concurrent AND-threads (all happen, any interleaving = SCXML `<parallel>`). L1+L2 stay EXCLUSIVE-per-playthrough = the tractable bundle the owner wants ("converge or not depending on the choice"); L3 is the genuinely-new AND-thread piece. The owner affirmed BOTH L1+L2 must be possible; they LAYER cleanly (structure -> conditions), so building L1 first is the foundation L2 sits on, not corner-painting.

STORY-GRAPH IS ALREADY VISUALIZABLE: report-fork-tree emits nodes (world-lines) + edges (fork parent + at) as text AND JSON — DEMONSTRATED live on the detroit 7-world-line store (Connor/Kara arcs, nested forks). A visual GUI (boxes+arrows via Mermaid/Graphviz/D3) is a SUBSTRATE-0 presentation layer (JS-presentation-allowed), unbuilt + YAGNI-gated on a consumer; the full visual = pinion (3D playable world). With confluence built, the diagram would show merges = the lattice = "which branches converge" made visible.

100-NPC DIALOGUE = a render PROJECTION of (personality entity-facts + the NPC's Frame/knowledge + the pointer's canon facts): different NPCs at one pointer differ by frame/personality, the same NPC across pointers differs by canon — a bounded (NPC x appearance x world-line) NODE matrix (node-not-path, scale-OK). The GATES keep N NPCs knowledge-consistent (a frame leak / a canon conflict is caught) = the worldbuilding-consistency-bible strength where a human author loses track. Honest: personality + knowledge must be AUTHORED as facts first (render voices, does not invent); voice distinctness = craft (R476 ceiling, blind-judged); runtime-reactive dialogue needs L2 gating (unbuilt) — pre-baked per-(NPC,scene,world-line) lines are doable now.

THROUGH-LINE: across every thread — render, SCXML compile, visualization, NPC dialogue — the projection/execution layers are PROVEN or CHEAP (substrate-0 / pinion-gated); the ONE binding bottleneck is AI SELF-AUTHORING at scale + breadth + coherence (premise -> a large, consistent, distinct-personality, branching+converging fact base). R524 proved 70-scene coherence; open-world breadth (100 NPCs / 10 villages / free-order) is the adjacent untested frontier. Every "can we do X" reduced to "render yes; the real test is whether self-authoring covers it."
verification_bullets: This is a CAPTURE round (the R518/R527 scope-a-frontier pattern), code 0 — it RECORDS the owner's post-R528 North-Star dialogue as a frontier map; no decision committed, no build, no substrate change.

Grounded LIVE, not asserted: report-fork-tree run on claudedocs/phase1-detroit-poc/detroit.atomic.json emitted 7 world-lines + nested forks (Connor/Kara) as text + JSON = the visualization graph data already exists. report-fork-tree / report-playthrough-manuscript / linearize confirmed as the facts->artifact codegen precedent (the SCXML target is one more of the same). The render-reads-facts-not-SCXML claim is consistent with the R514/R515 proven warm render (facts-only) + the CQRS pointer-projection memory.

Citations verified present before writing (R495 facts-first, R514/R515 warm render, R524 scale, R527 open-world gaps, R528 convergence-JOIN); naming-guard carried (narrative JOIN != persistence convergence-B; the SCXML seam is a NEW open question, NOT folded into convergence-JOIN).
append-only over R527/R528 (the R450/R498/R526 discipline); validate-workspace clean; design-doc sec 7.31 = the working-scratch detail, the R529 ledger = the committed SSOT.
carry_forward_bullets: RECOMMENDED #1 NEXT (the owner asked for the single top priority): TEST-1 = a CONVERGENCE-AUTHORING PROBE on the CURRENT forest substrate (code 0, experiment-gated, NO build). Brief a blind author with a premise REQUIRING many world-lines to converge into a shared ending (the R528 trigger), author on today's substrate, and MEASURE: does the author reach for a join? does the forced workaround (duplicate the ending / cross-path references) trip the R488/R522 gates? does it stay coherent? It is the SMALLEST test that closes the LARGEST open decision (build confluence or confirm YAGNI), and it is exactly the R528-designed trigger.

ROUTING (pre-committed, the R528 decision rule): workaround clearly damages -> PULL the (a) confluence build, then build + re-run = the full fork->join->fork->join validation. Workaround fine / author never reaches for convergence -> YAGNI confirmed, convergence-JOIN stays deferred, pivot to the breadth bottleneck.

RUNNER-UP / follow-on (NOT #1): the NPC-breadth probe (author 1 village x 5 distinct-personality, knowledge-distinct NPCs -> render dialogue -> leak/conflict gates) = the binding-bottleneck test on the open-world shape; sequence AFTER the structural substrate (forest vs confluence) is settled, since converging worlds want confluence.

Lower / open (unchanged): (b) the SCXML-seam scope round + the concurrency/quest-graph (L3) scope round; (c) v3 A-1/A-4; the story-map visualizer (substrate-0 presentation, pull-gated); the next authoring run instantiates the R526 sec-7.28 precondition clause.

Round 530 — convergence-probe/v1 (TEST-1.decision_summary: TEST-1, the R528-designed convergence-JOIN build TRIGGER, recommended #1-next by R529, authored + sha-pinned (R531 executes). It converts R528's analytic 'a cross-world-line JOIN is unexpressible on the forest' into a measured test on a REAL authoring attempt: a blind author authors a story whose structure REQUIRES convergence (the fresh 'Reckoning at Harlow Mill' premise: ONE exclusive midpoint fork -> two genuinely distinct worlds -> ONE shared dawn reckoning + river's-edge that BOTH must reach, the reckoning's content depending on the path) using ONLY the current forest substrate, and the orchestrator measures whether the post-fork shared continuation forces a damaging workaround. Deterministic measurement M1-M5 (duplication tax / R488-R522 gate trips / fork-tree-tree-vs-story-diamond / author friction / convergence dodge); pre-committed routing OUTCOME-1 or 1b (pull the confluence build, record the tax magnitude) vs OUTCOME-2 (faithful zero-duplication convergence => YAGNI confirmed, pivot to the NPC-breadth probe). The brief is the SAME proven R520/R524 top-down contract with NO convergence instruction added (bias guard) — the premise carries the convergence requirement as a story requirement only. Manifest sha256 46df8c997b0661703258f5a47f1dea84e261af21d069e03a10847878692fa2ce pinned pre-execution (the R503/R514/R519/R523 discipline); contamination bound R469 (the lineage that designed the model may not author the instance that tests it).
changes_bullets: convergence-probe/v1 = TEST-1, the R528 convergence-JOIN build TRIGGER probe (R529's recommended #1-next): does a convergence-REQUIRING story force a damaging workaround on the CURRENT forest substrate? Authored + sha-pinned the manifest, premise, and author-brief; the blind author runs in R531.

PREMISE (fresh, original — "The Reckoning at Harlow Mill"): a storm-night story whose dramatic structure makes convergence UNAVOIDABLE — ONE exclusive midpoint fork (open the sluice / hold and ride) producing two genuinely distinct worlds, both of which MUST reach ONE shared dawn reckoning (Garrick's one verdict) + ONE shared river's-edge image, where the reckoning's content DEPENDS on which path the night took (the post-fork shared continuation that the forest cannot natively host).

BRIEF = the SAME proven top-down contract used at R520/R524 (skeleton tentpoles -> detail fill; structural backreferences via evidence; the full gate list), trimmed to ~16-20 scenes; NO convergence instruction added (that would bias) — the premise carries the convergence requirement as a STORY requirement only, so the author meets the forest's gap on its own.

MEASUREMENT (deterministic, orchestrator-run): M1 duplication tax (facts/scenes duplicated across both terminal branches to realize the shared ending), M2 gate trips (R488 fact_canon_off_branch / R522 evidence_unreachable firing on a shared-ending backreference), M3 fork-tree-vs-story-shape (the story is a DIAMOND, the forest can only emit a TREE = the representation gap), M4 author friction (does the author-log note the awkwardness, or silently duplicate?), M5 convergence dodge (did the author flatten/split the shared ending to avoid the post-fork-sharing problem?).

DECISION RULE (pre-committed): OUTCOME-1 hard pull (duplication / gate-trips / tree-not-diamond) -> PULL the (a) confluence build, record the tax magnitude; OUTCOME-1b soft pull (the author dodged convergence) -> still a pull, a structural/craft tax; OUTCOME-2 YAGNI confirmed (faithful shared convergence, zero duplication, no dodge) -> defer the build, pivot to the NPC-breadth probe.

CONTRACT REUSE = no new tooling: reuses mnemosyne-cli verbs verbatim (the R520/R524 author-loop contract + the R522 evidence gate); scratch *.atomic.json gitignored, the sources (manifest/premise/brief, and in R531 the authored sections/facts/order + author-log + report) tracked.
verification_bullets: PRE-EXECUTION PIN (the R453/R499/R503/R514/R519/R523 discipline): manifest.json sha256 = 46df8c997b0661703258f5a47f1dea84e261af21d069e03a10847878692fa2ce, recorded HERE before the blind author runs (R531). Any post-hoc edit to the question / measurement / decision rule changes this hash and is detectable.

CONTAMINATION BOUND (R469, the 8th use of the blind-experiment family): the lineage that scoped (R527) + designed (R528) the convergence-JOIN model may NOT author the instance that tests whether it is needed. The blind author gets ONLY premise.md + author-brief.md, BLIND to the hypothesis / measurement / routing; the brief adds no convergence instruction (the premise carries it as story only).

HONEST framing recorded in the manifest: R528 already proved the join analytically unexpressible, so this probe does NOT re-derive existence — it measures the decision-relevant unknowns (does real authoring NEED convergence, how big is the duplication tax, does the loop NOTICE or dodge). The pre-registered expectation is OUTCOME-1, with a genuine OUTCOME-2 flagged as the high-value surprise that would confirm YAGNI.

Round 531 — convergence-probe/v1 (TEST-1.decision_summary: TEST-1 executed and routed cleanly to OUTCOME-1 (hard pull). A blind fresh-context author (R469 bound), given ONLY the premise + the proven R520/R524 top-down contract with NO convergence instruction, authored an 18-scene / 39-fact / 2-world-line convergence story ('The Reckoning at Harlow Mill') on the current forest substrate. The orchestrator rebuilt the store fresh and measured: M1 the shared ending = 6 story beats authored as 12 facts (exact 2x duplication, parallel a/b sets, because a fact carries exactly one branch); M2 0 gate trips (the author was funneled to duplication, avoiding the cross-branch refs that trip R488/R522); M3 report-fork-tree shows a TREE (the sc-08 divergence only), not the story's DIAMOND (it cannot show the sc-reckoning merge); M4 the blind author independently rediscovered and named the exact R528 finding ('the fork model is a forest ... there is no native join node ... convergence is expressed, not declared ... a reader of branches[] alone would not see it ... duplication is unavoidable'); M5 convergence MET (not dodged), via duplication. The result FIELD-VALIDATES R528's code finding (the canon-order already tolerated a shared tail node, gate-clean = the order algebra is DAG-general; the gap is the branch-registry declaration + the one-branch-per-fact rule, exactly where R528 placed it) and confirms the confluence design is aimed correctly. Per the R530 pre-committed rule, OUTCOME-1 PULLS the convergence-JOIN build; the measured tax it removes = the whole shared continuation duplicated 2x plus the structural invisibility plus the silent-drift maintenance cost. The contamination bound held (the lineage designed the model but authored nothing; a blind author rediscovering the gap is the strongest evidence it is real).
changes_bullets: TEST-1 EXECUTED blind: ONE fresh-context author (R469 bound), given ONLY the premise + the proven R520/R524 contract (NO convergence instruction), authored an 18-scene / 39-fact / 2-world-line convergence story ("The Reckoning at Harlow Mill": ONE fork at sc-08 -> sluice/ride -> both reach the shared sc-reckoning -> sc-river). The orchestrator rebuilt the store fresh from the author's sources and ran every gate independently.

RESULT = OUTCOME-1 (hard pull). M1 duplication tax: the shared ending = 6 story beats authored as 12 facts = EXACT 2x duplication (f-300a==f-300b "the town gathers in the mill yard", f-400a==f-400b "Sela walks to the river's edge", etc.) — the whole shared continuation written once per branch because a fact carries one branch. M2 gate trips = 0 (the author was FUNNELED to duplication, avoiding the cross-branch refs that would trip R488/R522). M3 = TREE not diamond (report-fork-tree shows only the sc-08 divergence; it cannot show the sc-reckoning merge). M5 = convergence MET (not dodged), via duplication.

M4 (the headline): the BLIND author — zero knowledge of the convergence-JOIN hypothesis — independently rediscovered the exact R528 finding and named it: "the fork model is a forest (each branch has a single parent), so there is no native 'join' node ... convergence is expressed, not declared: nothing in the registries says 'these two branches join here' ... A reader of branches[] alone would not see it ... the same dawn beat had to be authored as TWO parallel fact sets ... That duplication is unavoidable given the model: a fact belongs to exactly one branch."

R528 DESIGN field-validated + refined (append-only): the author found that sharing a scene-id at the tail of BOTH branch orders (sc-reckoning in both chains) is "legal and clean" — the canon-ORDER already TOLERATES a shared tail node, gate-clean. This confirms R528's core code finding (the order algebra is already DAG-general and did not resist); the gap is exactly where R528 placed it — the BRANCH REGISTRY cannot DECLARE the merge (invisible to report-fork-tree/branches[]) and a FACT carries one branch (so the shared content must be DUPLICATED). The confluence design targets precisely these two, not the order algebra = aimed correctly.

Secondary (orthogonal to convergence, recorded for a future look): report-payoff-substantiation forced the author to re-model "Sela IS the keeper" (a constant) as a transition contested->affirmed to discharge a typed setup->payoff — a tool-shaping pressure on how a true constant is stated.

DECISION (pre-committed R530 rule): OUTCOME-1 => PULL the convergence-JOIN BUILD (R528 confluence: multi-parent branch ancestry + the per-parent "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence" gate + bounded cross-confluence succession). The measured tax the build removes = the whole shared ending duplicated 2x (6 beats / 12 facts) + the structural invisibility (fork-tree cannot show the merge) + the silent-drift maintenance cost (nothing links f-300a to f-300b). After the build, re-run THIS premise on the new substrate = the full fork->join validation.
verification_bullets: PIN HELD: the R530 manifest sha256 (46df8c99...) was committed before the author ran; the question / measurement / decision rule were fixed pre-execution and not edited post-hoc.

ORCHESTRATOR INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION: the measurement did NOT trust the author's own store — the orchestrator rebuilt a fresh store from the author's sections.json + facts.json + order.json (import-sections + import-facts, 0 errors, 18 sections / 39 facts / 2 branches) and ran validate-continuity (0/0), report-fork-tree (2 world-lines, 0 unplaced, NO merge node), report-payoff-coverage (terminal worlds clean) itself. The M1 duplication count (12 facts at sc-reckoning+sc-river, 6 per branch, parallel a/b pairs) was computed directly from facts.json.

CONTAMINATION BOUND held (R469, 8th use): this lineage designed the convergence-JOIN model + wrote the premise/brief but authored NO fact and made no creative choice; the blind author was a separate fresh-context subagent with only premise.md + author-brief.md. The independent rediscovery of the "no native join node" finding by a blind author is the strongest evidence the gap is real and not lineage-suggested.

VALIDATION after the build: re-run convergence-probe/v1's SAME premise on the new substrate (convergence-probe/v2) — the shared ending authored ONCE (not 6 beats x2), the merge visible in report-fork-tree, the new gate exercised = the full fork->join validation, and the measured tax (12 -> 6 facts) confirmed removed.
RUNNER-UP (still queued, lower): the NPC-breadth probe (the binding-bottleneck test on the open-world breadth shape).

Round 532 — convergence-JOIN build step.decision_summary: Step 1 of the R528/sec-7.30 convergence-JOIN build, pull-justified by R531's OUTCOME-1 (the blind author was forced to duplicate the shared ending 2x and named the gap 'convergence is expressed, not declared; a reader of branches[] alone would not see it'). This round makes a world-line MERGE declarable + structurally visible WITHOUT touching the load-bearing visibility/holds_at predicate (no semantic risk): Branch gains converges_from: Vec<BranchFork> (the inverse of forks_from — the parents that converge into this shared continuation), with the mutate primitive validating >= 2 distinct pre-existing parents + real merge-section coordinates + the fork-XOR-confluence rule (a single shared build_branch_candidate for add_branch + import-facts); report-fork-tree's ForkTreeBranch gains a converges array, surfacing the merge with each coordinate resolved vs the parent's composed order. CLI --converges <parent>=<section> (repeatable) + MCP converges_from carry it; schema 22->23. End-to-end CLI smoke proven (declare a 2-parent confluence, fork-tree shows both merges). The fact-sharing composition (author once, both parent worlds inherit — removes the measured 2x duplication tax) and the per-parent 'no unreconciled dependency across a confluence' gate are the explicit next steps (R533/R534), not half-done here. Full atomic+validate suites green; clippy -D + fmt green.
changes_bullets: The convergence-JOIN BUILD, step 1 of R528/sec 7.30 (pull-justified by R531's OUTCOME-1): make a world-line MERGE declarable + structurally visible — the exact gap R531's blind author named ("convergence is expressed, not declared; a reader of branches[] alone would not see it"). Code round, the first in this arc.

SCHEMA (mnemosyne-core narrative.rs): Branch gains `converges_from: Vec<BranchFork>` (skip-if-empty, byte-stable), the inverse of `forks_from` — the parents that CONVERGE into this shared continuation, each {branch, at} = a parent + its merge coordinate. CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 22 -> 23.

MUTATE (mnemosyne-atomic): new `build_branch_converges` (mirrors build_branch_fork — each parent must pre-exist + be a registered/main branch, each merge point a real section, no self-merge, no duplicate parent, >= 2 parents since a 1-parent merge is just a fork) + a shared `build_branch_candidate` enforcing the fork-XOR-confluence rule (a world-line is a fork-child OR a confluence, never both); add_branch gains a `converges_from: &[(&str,&str)]` arg; import-facts BranchImport gains a `converges_from: Vec<BranchConvergeImport>` field (mutually exclusive with forks). Divergent-reject covers converges_from (Branch derives PartialEq).

SURFACE (mnemosyne-validate report-fork-tree): ForkTreeBranch gains `converges: Vec<ForkTreeEdge>`; fork_tree populates it (same parent-registered fail-loud as the fork side; each merge coordinate resolved vs the PARENT's composed order via CanonOrder::names; unplaced merge points land in unplaced_fork_points, deduped). The merge the fork tree alone could never show is now declared and visible.

CARRIERS: CLI `add-branch --converges <parent>=<merge-section>` (repeatable); the report-fork-tree text/JSON shows "X converges from Y at Z" (a confluence no longer mis-prints as "standalone world"); MCP add_branch gains `converges_from: [{branch, at}]`. End-to-end CLI smoke proven: declare a 2-parent confluence, report-fork-tree shows both incoming merges with at_placed=true.

SCOPE (this step): convergence is now DECLARABLE + VISIBLE. NOT yet built (the next steps): the visibility/order-composition that lets a confluence's facts be authored ONCE and SHARED by both parent worlds (removes R531's 2x duplication tax), and the per-parent "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence" gate. The load-bearing visibility/holds_at predicate is UNCHANGED this round (no semantic risk); a confluence with no facts on it is a valid structural declaration.

TESTS: add_branch_confluence_validation (>=2 parents / pre-exist / sections / dup / fork-XOR-confluence / idempotent+divergent round-trip) + fork_tree_surfaces_confluence_merges (both merges surfaced, parents + at_placed). Full atomic (184) + validate (253) suites green; clippy -D + fmt green.
verification_bullets: BUILD + TESTS green: cargo build clean; full test suites mnemosyne-atomic 184 passed / mnemosyne-validate 253 passed (incl. the 2 new tests); clippy -D warnings clean on all 6 touched crates (core/atomic/validate/cli/mcp/ops); cargo fmt applied.

Round 532 — convergence-JOIN build step.publishable_decision_summary: Step 1 of the R528/sec-7.30 convergence-JOIN build, pull-justified by R531's OUTCOME-1 (the blind author was forced to duplicate the shared ending 2x and named the gap 'convergence is expressed, not declared; a reader of branches[] alone would not see it'). This round makes a world-line MERGE declarable + structurally visible WITHOUT touching the load-bearing visibility/holds_at predicate (no semantic risk): Branch gains converges_from: Vec<BranchFork> (the inverse of forks_from — the parents that converge into this shared continuation), with the mutate primitive validating >= 2 distinct pre-existing parents + real merge-section coordinates + the fork-XOR-confluence rule (a single shared build_branch_candidate for add_branch + import-facts); report-fork-tree's ForkTreeBranch gains a converges array, surfacing the merge with each coordinate resolved vs the parent's composed order. CLI --converges <parent>=<section> (repeatable) + MCP converges_from carry it; schema 22->23. End-to-end CLI smoke proven (declare a 2-parent confluence, fork-tree shows both merges). The fact-sharing composition (author once, both parent worlds inherit — removes the measured 2x duplication tax) and the per-parent 'no unreconciled dependency across a confluence' gate are the explicit next steps (R533/R534), not half-done here. Full atomic+validate suites green; clippy -D + fmt green.
publishable_changes_bullets: The convergence-JOIN BUILD, step 1 of R528/sec 7.30 (pull-justified by R531's OUTCOME-1): make a world-line MERGE declarable + structurally visible — the exact gap R531's blind author named ("convergence is expressed, not declared; a reader of branches[] alone would not see it"). Code round, the first in this arc.

SCHEMA (mnemosyne-core narrative.rs): Branch gains `converges_from: Vec<BranchFork>` (skip-if-empty, byte-stable), the inverse of `forks_from` — the parents that CONVERGE into this shared continuation, each {branch, at} = a parent + its merge coordinate. CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 22 -> 23.

MUTATE (mnemosyne-atomic): new `build_branch_converges` (mirrors build_branch_fork — each parent must pre-exist + be a registered/main branch, each merge point a real section, no self-merge, no duplicate parent, >= 2 parents since a 1-parent merge is just a fork) + a shared `build_branch_candidate` enforcing the fork-XOR-confluence rule (a world-line is a fork-child OR a confluence, never both); add_branch gains a `converges_from: &[(&str,&str)]` arg; import-facts BranchImport gains a `converges_from: Vec<BranchConvergeImport>` field (mutually exclusive with forks). Divergent-reject covers converges_from (Branch derives PartialEq).

SURFACE (mnemosyne-validate report-fork-tree): ForkTreeBranch gains `converges: Vec<ForkTreeEdge>`; fork_tree populates it (same parent-registered fail-loud as the fork side; each merge coordinate resolved vs the PARENT's composed order via CanonOrder::names; unplaced merge points land in unplaced_fork_points, deduped). The merge the fork tree alone could never show is now declared and visible.

CARRIERS: CLI `add-branch --converges <parent>=<merge-section>` (repeatable); the report-fork-tree text/JSON shows "X converges from Y at Z" (a confluence no longer mis-prints as "standalone world"); MCP add_branch gains `converges_from: [{branch, at}]`. End-to-end CLI smoke proven: declare a 2-parent confluence, report-fork-tree shows both incoming merges with at_placed=true.

SCOPE (this step): convergence is now DECLARABLE + VISIBLE. NOT yet built (the next steps): the visibility/order-composition that lets a confluence's facts be authored ONCE and SHARED by both parent worlds (removes R531's 2x duplication tax), and the per-parent "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence" gate. The load-bearing visibility/holds_at predicate is UNCHANGED this round (no semantic risk); a confluence with no facts on it is a valid structural declaration.

TESTS: add_branch_confluence_validation (>=2 parents / pre-exist / sections / dup / fork-XOR-confluence / idempotent+divergent round-trip) + fork_tree_surfaces_confluence_merges (both merges surfaced, parents + at_placed). Full atomic (184) + validate (253) suites green; clippy -D + fmt green.
